description: English mezmur chants for practice (chant workshop).
implementationNotes: Each entry matches MezmurEntry in src/data/types/mezmur.ts.

entries.title: Cana of Galilee
entries.lyrics:
Cana of Galilee /x2/ in the matrimony
Virgin we have found you with divinity 
Our Lord we have found you with our Lady

Invited were the crowds flooding through the foyer
Celebrating they ate emptied the wine chamber 
Our Holy Virgin the Mother of our Lord 
For your mediation it is you they implored

With you in attendance ashamed they will not feel
Nothing is beneath you fill with wine their barrel
“They have no wine” you said to your son our Lord 
It is our Mother we cherish and adore 

Divinity of Lord that was unveiled to us
How fortunate it was the house of Dokimas 
And today in the home of this grand unity 
Blessings have spilled to us, with His humility 

Transfigured was the wine when it was just water 
In Cana Galilee our eyes did not falter 
If we ensure that He remains within our midst
Good wine will overflow as written in the scripts
entries.meaning: This mezmur reflects the miracle of Christ at Cana of Galilee, where He turned water into wine during a wedding. It highlights the intercession of the Holy Virgin Mary, her role in presenting the need to Christ, and the blessing that comes when Christ is present in a home. The chant emphasizes faith, divine power, humility, and the overflowing blessings that come through God’s presence.

entries.title: I can't do it alone
entries.lyrics:
I can't do it alone, Lord be my strength /x2/
My soul longs for you, show me your truth /x2/
Help me cry and regret my sins, Lord forgive me /x2/
Give me, a repentant heart with humility

Give me tears of repentance, The Tears of St. Peter
The tears of the woman who cried, washed, Your feet with her hair
Give me the tears of King David, and his humble heart
Let me cry to cleanse my sins, give me true regret...
I can't do it alone, Lord be my strength.
Chorus….

You are my hope and my Lord, shine your guiding light
I repent, confess, all my sins, alone I cannot fight
Help me to live righteously, give me a way out
I am empty without you, send me your spirit
I can't do it alone, Lord be my strength.
Chorus….

Grant me depth in knowing you, teach me the right path
Reveal, to me Your mysteries, please strengthen my faith
You suffered on my behalf, on the cross you died
You gave me Your flesh and blood, so I can be saved
I can't do it alone, Lord be my strength.
entries.meaning: This mezmur is a deep prayer of repentance and dependence on God. It expresses the believer’s inability to overcome sin alone and the need for God’s strength, mercy, and guidance. It references biblical figures like St. Peter, the sinful woman, and King David, highlighting true repentance through tears, humility, and confession. The chant emphasizes faith, confession, spiritual struggle, and the saving power of Christ through His sacrifice.

entries.title: Before the most high
entries.lyrics:
Before the most high I will fall within His dwelling place
And bring forth my praise to glorify His name

For all that He has done I will sing hymns for Him
Within His holy church bowing for all my sins
To stand before my Lord I will rise forgiven

Through my days of despair He's my shield and armor
Find me within the church singing to the Father
Sowing my seeds of praise the heavenly gardener

Praising like King David with a harp of 10 strings
Along with the angels with joyful noise I sing
My words my sacrifice accept my offerings

To the one and true God for His name I will kneel
Giving my tithes to Him promises I'll fulfill
To enter His kingdom it is my Savior's will

Your glory I declare as I proclaim in song
Praying in your temple before the rise of dawn
Along with my fathers to you I call upon

ወደ ማደሪያው ገብቼ ልስገድ ለእግዚአብሔር 
ምስጋናንም ላቅርብ ስለስሙ ክብር
entries.transliterationLyrics:
Wede maderyaw gebiche lisged le Egziabher
Misgananim laqrib sile simu kibir
entries.meaning: This mezmur expresses a life centered on worship, humility, and devotion to God. It speaks about entering the house of God with reverence, offering praise, repentance, and thanksgiving. The chant reflects the example of King David in worship, the importance of the church as a dwelling place of God, and the believer’s desire to live righteously, give offerings, and seek salvation. It emphasizes praise, sacrifice, and a continual relationship with God through prayer and worship.

entries.title: Believe In The Lord
entries.lyrics:
እመኑ በእርሱ ድንቅ ያደርጋል ጌታ
በረድ አዝንቦ  ጠላት እየመታ
ውቅያኖስ ጥልቁን ፈጥኖ ያተነነ
ነገር ለወጠ ሁሉ በእርሱ ሆነ 

Believe in the Lord, He works wonders and feats 
Sending hail down and striking his enemies 
By his might the depths, and the seas became haze
He changed the outcome, by Him all things were made  

Believe in the Lord, The mountain is ablaze 
Believe in the Lord, The valley disarranged
Believe in the Lord, The evergreen is crushed 
Believe in the Lord, and the rock ground to dust 
Believe in the Lord, the gentiles’ statues  
Believe in the Lord, before Him were no more 
Believe in the Lord, Those who invoked His name 
Believe in the Lord, sailed calmly through the storms 

Believe in the Lord, the sun will always rise
Believe in the Lord, every day becomes night
Believe in the Lord, He can perform all things
Believe in the Lord, our Savior, our Might
Believe in the Lord, to us He is Lord Love
Believe in the Lord, Father of creation
Believe in the Lord, He looks down from above
Believe in the Lord, His hands are stretched to us

Believe in the Lord, we have a sturdy boat
Believe in the Lord, no storm or waves can break
Believe in the Lord, so we won’t fear henceforth
Believe in the Lord, our lives He won’t forsake
Believe in the Lord, The enemy has lost 
Believe in the Lord, Satan is put to shame 
Believe in the Lord, let the prisons rejoice
Believe in the Lord, with joyful hymns of praise

Believe in the Lord, The crafty lies of foes
Believe in the Lord, are empty, have no might
Believe in the Lord, Emmanuel Logos
Believe in the Lord, is with us through our plight  
Believe in the Lord, when our Lord bestows 
Believe in the Lord, His grace which is plenty
Believe in the Lord, we will stand atop death
Believe in the Lord, singing hymns joyfully
entries.transliterationLyrics:
Emenu be'ersu dinik yadergal geta
Bered aznbo telat eyemeta
Wukiyanosi tilqun fet'no yatenena
Neger lewete hulu be'ersu hone
entries.meaning: This mezmur is a powerful declaration of faith in God’s absolute power and sovereignty. It recounts God’s miracles, His authority over nature, and His victory over enemies. The chant encourages unwavering belief in the Lord through all circumstances, emphasizing that He controls all things, protects His people, and grants victory over sin, fear, and death. It highlights faith, trust, divine power, and the assurance of salvation through Christ.

entries.title: By the Lord's grand design
entries.lyrics:
By the Lord's grand design all is rightly aligned
For all those who love Him blessings they will find

Bebego fekadu hulu bersu hone
Neger hulu bersu tekenawene

The Heavens and the Earth His splendor they convey
Every sunrise and night His grand work on display
From the whispers of winds to the oceans that leap
We honor exalt Him His promises we keep

From what came to exist each piece is His design
In Heaven and on Earth His wonders intertwine
The sovereign Lord decrees for the good and we're glad
In His grand creation all things good He has clad

The summer to winter and from daylight to night
He displays these wonders guiding our blinded sight
In His perfect canvas there’s no flaw to be rid
For there’s no one like Him our Creator is vivid

He feels our despair and joys that have lighted
Every twist every turn by His hand is guided
In joy and in sorrow His true purpose we find
For these eternal truths in God we will abide

By the Lord's grand design all is rightly aligned
For all those who love Him blessings they will find

Bebego fekadu hulu bersu hone
Neger hulu bersu tekenawene
entries.transliterationLyrics:
Bebego fekadu hulu bersu hone
Neger hulu bersu tekenawene
entries.meaning: This mezmur reflects the divine order and sovereignty of God over all creation. It emphasizes that everything in existence is part of God’s perfect plan and design, both in heaven and on earth. The chant reassures believers that in every season—joy or sorrow—God is guiding all things for good for those who love Him. It highlights trust in God’s will, the beauty of creation, and the peace found in relying on His perfect purpose.

entries.title: Come all let us rejoice
entries.lyrics:
Come all let us rejoice in the Lord Jesus Christ
From slavery of sin He freed us with his might
Arise with songs of praise His favor’s abundant 
There’s no way to repay but to be jubilant

Sewoch des yibelen beAmlakachin
Kehatiyat barinet netsa yawetan
Tenesu enamesgin wiletaw bizu new
Min yikefelewal temesgen bicha new

From my sickness of sin with chains I have been bound  
The Lord my medicine has raised me from the ground
Your gifts are plentiful that You bestow on me
All I can give to you is praise and melody

Just like Bartimeus my eyes they could not see
But now through the Lord’s Grace His healing I received 
I’m guided by His laws on my path they will shine
Since I belong to Him His peace has become mine 

With a proclamation showing your love to me
I was chained by demons but with you now I’m free
Alpha and Omega for all eternity
You are El Shaddai God Emmanuel we call Thee

The contract of my death to save me He has ripped
The wonders He performed with my eyes I witnessed 
His Grace never-ending my God the almighty 
We glorify Your name out loud in jubilee

Come all let us rejoice in the Lord Jesus Christ
From slavery of sin He freed us with his might
Arise with songs of praise His favor’s abundant 
There’s no way to repay but to be jubilant

Sewoch des yibelen beAmlakachin
Kehatiyat barinet netsa yawetan
Tenesu enamesgin wiletaw bizu new
Min yikefelewal temesgen bicha new
entries.transliterationLyrics:
Sewoch des yibelen beAmlakachin
Kehatiyat barinet netsa yawetan
Tenesu enamesgin wiletaw bizu new
Min yikefelewal temesgen bicha new
entries.meaning: This mezmur is a joyful proclamation of salvation through Jesus Christ. It celebrates freedom from sin, healing, and the abundant grace of God. The chant reflects personal transformation, referencing biblical healing such as Bartimaeus, and emphasizes gratitude, praise, and rejoicing. It highlights Christ as Savior, healer, and eternal God, calling believers to respond with thanksgiving and joyful worship.

entries.title: Covenant of Mercy
entries.lyrics:
For Covenant of Mercy patiently I waited
I was consoled all my sorrows faded
Mother of God our Lady Mary
For all my days bestow grace upon me 

Dej tenahu qoyiche Kidane Mehretin
Tetsnanahugn resahu hazenen
Yamlak enat Emebetachin
Moges hugnign qeriw zemenen

The times of misery, with ease I left behind
Drawing nearer to you, redemption of mankind
The grace of your Child and your benevolence
Protected your servant from a worrisome death

My mother, as I called your name
All of my torments I overcame
In front of, you tears fell down my face
You dried them, for I’m in your embrace
Peace be unto you

My heart grew firm by you and it became lifted
Upon my enemies, my mouth spoke convicted
By your healing wonders, your servant is happy
Your child has broken arrows of the mighty

My mother, as I called your name
All of my torments I overcame
In front of, you tears fell down my face
You dried them, for I’m in your embrace
Peace be unto you

My loved ones fled when my hands weren’t full and sufficed
When they tried to divide with their cursed advice
I told them that He had taken what He first gave
But I have my Lady, they were quickly ashamed

My mother, as I called your name
All of my torments I overcame
In front of, you tears fell down my face
You dried them, for I’m in your embrace
Peace be unto you

As I stood on the stage, calling her name to praise
With gentle compassion, towards me she had gazed
No one mirrors your rare and unique thoughtfulness
Through you Mother Mary, I found my true solace

Enate simshin sitera 
Alefe ya hulu mekera
Enbaye kefitish fesese
Emiye banchi yetabese
Selam leki

Dej tenahu qoyiche Kidane Mehretin
Tetsnanahugn resahu hazenen
Yamlak enat Emebetachin
Moges hugnign qeriw zemenen

For Covenant of Mercy patiently I waited
I was consoled all my sorrows faded
Mother of God our Lady Mary
For all my days bestow grace upon me

entries.meaning: This mezmur is a heartfelt devotion to the Holy Virgin Mary under the title of Kidane Mehret (Covenant of Mercy). It expresses patience, suffering, and ultimate consolation through her intercession. The chant reflects the believer’s reliance on the Mother of God during hardship, emphasizing her compassion, protection, and healing power. It highlights themes of endurance, faith, and the peace and comfort found through her prayers, leading to restoration and spiritual strength.

entries.title: Emmanuel
entries.lyrics:
Emmanuel He’s loving and forgiving
Fenced by mercy in Heaven He is dwelling
Calling upon His Father while He’s in pain
Those who nailed Him to the Cross He didn’t blame

አማኑኤል ይቅር ባይነው ፍቅር   
ምህረት ከቦት በሰማያት ቢኖር
እያመመዉ አባቱንን የጠራ
አይቻለዉ ለሚገድሉት ሲራራ

Believe that He is merciful for He is not a ruthless king
His sanctified and precious Blood will cleanse us from sin endlessly
With a grand wave of divine grace the Earth has been filled and flooded
His mercy as His source of strength from death we have been protected

The God who enlightened the dark for brighter days He paved the way
His spear of love pierces us all without it we are left astray
Trials we passed with victory overcoming all with Jesus
Our sins have been covered by Christ the Lord who died for all of us

There’s not a single sin greater than our Lord’s eternal love
We shouldn’t fear or hesitate to reunite with God above
The heavens as our homeland no one will fall or be ignored
For it is our inheritance by the Holy Blood of our Lord

In all the heavens and the Earth no one shows His dedication
His Holy name is Jesus Christ with Him He brought us affection
Above the grave He has risen His love left the world astonished
On His shoulders He carried us the children that He had cherished

Emmanuel He’s loving and forgiving
Fenced by mercy in Heaven He is dwelling
Calling upon His Father while He’s in pain
Those who nailed Him to the Cross He didn’t blame

አማኑኤል ይቅር ባይነው ፍቅር   
ምህረት ከቦት በሰማያት ቢኖር
እያመመዉ አባቱንን የጠራ
አይቻለዉ ለሚገድሉት ሲራራ
entries.transliterationLyrics:
Amanuel yiqir baynew fiqir
Mihret kebot besemayat binor
Eyammemew abatun yenetara
Ayichalew lemigedlut sirara
entries.meaning: This mezmur reflects the boundless love and mercy of Jesus Christ (Emmanuel), emphasizing His forgiveness even during His suffering on the Cross. It highlights His sacrifice, the cleansing power of His blood, and His victory over death. The chant calls believers to trust in His mercy, overcome fear, and draw closer to God through repentance and faith. It celebrates Christ’s love, redemption, and the eternal hope of salvation through Him.

entries.title: For My Eyes
entries.lyrics:
For my eyes have witnessed the Lord’s salvation (2)
Honor and praise (2) we give with devotion

Aynoche madanun aytewalina (2)
Yigebawal (2) kibir ena misgana

Descended from heaven for He so loved the world
The gracious Almighty Jesus Christ our Lord
Hidden in Bethlehem inside of the manger
He was born on that day our God and Savior

Many were the prophets that told of His coming
Born from Virgin Mary saved us from suffering
Lifted us from the dust path of iniquity
Saying hallelujah let’s sing in unity

Desired to restore those who were cast away
Honored us with His love in lowliness He came
What kind of love is this everlasting mercy
Our God being born in a barn so humbly

Remained perfectly pure in the sanctuary
Gave birth to salvation with full virginity
The Lord that rids the world of evil transgression
The hope of all mankind was born from the Virgin
entries.transliterationLyrics:
Aynoche madanun aytewalina (2)
Yigebawal (2) kibir ena misgana
entries.meaning: This mezmur proclaims the joy of witnessing the salvation of the Lord, focusing on the birth of Jesus Christ. It reflects on God’s love for humanity, fulfilled through the Incarnation, where Christ was born humbly in Bethlehem. The chant highlights the fulfillment of prophecy, the role of the Virgin Mary, and the restoration of humanity from sin. It emphasizes praise, unity, and gratitude for the gift of salvation brought into the world.

entries.title: For There’s No One Like Him
entries.lyrics:
For There's No One Like Him Our God the almighty /x2/
Arise for Praise and sing with jumble /x2/ 

እንደ እግዚአብሔር ያለ ማንም የለምና
እልል በሉ ቁሙ ለምስጋና /፪/

The water was parted revealing the sea floor, The pharaoh had fallen his screaming was no more, the meek used the new path and had treaded, the powerful were humiliated
Chorus….

The walls of Jericho untouched by any hand, What seemed invincible has crumbled down to sand, Though the mighty may seem to grow in strength, we’ll remain firm in him we have our faith 
Chorus…. 

Our adversary had thrown a piercing rod, it tumbled to the ground were shielded by our God, let our praise soar to the trinity, for he has lost the hunting enemy
Chorus….

On the sea he strides with grace and the utmost ease, when he rises grandly the violent winds will cease, we worship him the Virgin Mary’s son, he’s merciful impossible he’s done, he’s merciful impossible he’s done. 

እንደ እግዚአብሔር ያለ ማንም የለምና/x2/
እልል በሉ ቁሙ ለምስጋና /x2/

For There's No One Like Him Our God the almighty /x2/
Arise for Praise and sing with jumble /x2/
entries.transliterationLyrics:
Ende Egziabher yalew manim yelemna
Ellil belu kumu lemisgana
entries.meaning: This mezmur proclaims the unmatched power and greatness of God, declaring that there is no one like Him. It reflects on His mighty works throughout history, such as parting the Red Sea and bringing down Jericho, showing His authority over all creation and enemies. The chant calls believers to stand firm in faith, rejoice, and praise God for His victories, protection, and miracles. It emphasizes divine power, faith, and joyful worship.

entries.title: From the scorching flames
entries.lyrics:
From the scorching flames and burning fire (2)
He swiftly removed me the Angel Gabriel my rescuer (2)

ከሚነደው እሳት ነበልባል (2)
ያወጣኝ እርሱ ነው የታደገኝ ፈጥኖ ገብርኤል (2)

Distressed was the spirit of Nebuchadnezzar
For this he desired a dream interpreter 
The false magic of the Chaldeans (Kal-dee-yuns) was exposed
Through God’s intervention Gabriel delivered me from being disposed (2)

From the scorching flames and burning fire (2)
He swiftly removed me the Angel Gabriel my rescuer (2)

Leader of the captives Ashpenaz (exactly how it looks) was amazed
By the edge of the fire he saw me while I praised
Though I’m a child of God the king didn’t notice
Our guardian angel with his might preserved us from the blazing furnace

From the scorching flames and burning fire (2)
He swiftly removed me the Angel Gabriel my rescuer (2)

The enemy that induced trials lit the torch
Although my body was burned my soul was not scorched
Survived the Babylonian fire through aid
The servant of the Lord with his pleas my father provided me with shade (2)

From the scorching flames and burning fire (2)
He swiftly removed me the Angel Gabriel my rescuer (2)

I won’t succumb though they scare with boiling water
The world may burn in fire but my faith won’t falter
Like he saved Qirqos and Eyeluta from heat
He parted heaven to speedily save me from the ultimate defeat (2)

From the scorching flames and burning fire (2)
He swiftly removed me the Angel Gabriel my rescuer (2)

ከሚነደው እሳት ነበልባል (2)
ያወጣኝ እርሱ ነው የታደገኝ ፈጥኖ ገብርኤል (2)
entries.transliterationLyrics:
Keminedew isat nebelbal (2)
Yawotagn ersu new yetadegagn fet'no Gebriel (2)
entries.meaning: This mezmur reflects God’s deliverance from trials and dangers, especially through the intercession of the Archangel Gabriel. It draws from the biblical story of the fiery furnace and emphasizes divine protection even in the midst of suffering. The chant highlights faith that remains unshaken despite trials, the power of angels as servants of God, and victory over persecution. It also connects to saints who endured suffering and were saved by God’s grace.

entries.title: Gabriel the powerful
entries.lyrics:
Gabriel the powerful, the angel of peace and of the good news
You are rescuing, the people of God, from the fire that burns 
Your love is written, deep within our hearts 
We stand before you, so you can bless us 

አዝ==
The stories were told of their resilience,
While in Babylon, the city of death 
Even being so young, their faith was shining
St. Gabriel you stood, you were protecting.

አዝ==
When the water boiled, it caused so much fear.
When death and their pain, seemed like it was near. 
They prayed and they sang, the mother and child
By the fiery place, when you had arrived 

አዝ==
Cyricus had hope, never fearing death 
Because St Gabriel, with them you were close
The son told his mom, do not be afraid 
This difficult trial, through faith they had passed 

አዝ==
I also believe that you will save me,
Intercede to Christ, who loves patiently 
So I cross over past this awful time 
please give me the strength, so I overcome
entries.meaning: This mezmur honors the Archangel Gabriel as a powerful protector and bearer of good news. It reflects on his role in delivering and protecting the faithful, especially in times of trial and persecution. The chant references biblical and traditional stories of endurance, including the protection of the righteous in fire and the faith of saints like Cyricus. It emphasizes intercession, courage, and trust in God’s help through His angels.

entries.title: Go forth testify
entries.lyrics:
Go forth testify tell the whole world He’s risen can’t be conformed our Savior and Lord
The incapable death He destroyed with His might we are restored
Released us from the seal’s hold the resurrected Lord

ሂዱ ንገሩ አውሩ ለዓለም ተነሥቷል በዚህ የለም መድኃኔዓለም
ሞት የማይችለው የበረታ ኃያል ነው የማይረታ
ማኅተሙን የፈታ የትንሣኤው ጌታ

Oppressive death with His victory is defeated
Removed my chains and replaced with the harp of David
Saying O death where’s your sting we sing in unison
Your Holy Cross and name serve as our protection
We declare salvation

Go forth testify tell the whole world He’s risen can’t be conformed our Savior and Lord
The incapable death He destroyed with His might we are restored
Released us from the seal’s hold the resurrected Lord

With Your guidance and eternal aid we have crossed through
Demise could not confine You to the burial tomb
The merciless with Your might they are now imprisoned
We praise You Lord for Your success is never halted
You shall be exalted

Go forth testify tell the whole world He’s risen can’t be conformed our Savior and Lord
The incapable death He destroyed with His might we are restored
Released us from the seal’s hold the resurrected Lord

Aside from You who can die in order to redeem
Through witnesses Your virtuous works will always gleam
My beloved Your beauty is radiant and beams
Dismay and fear were instilled into our enemies
Marvelous are Your deeds

Go forth testify tell the whole world He’s risen can’t be conformed our Savior and Lord
The incapable death He destroyed with His might we are restored
Released us from the seal’s hold the resurrected Lord

Proclaim this news for it is above all miracles
For this triumph dressed in white are His holy angels
The city shook and had awakened during the night
The hymnody made the prison tremble out of fright
Play the drums in delight

Go forth testify tell the whole world He’s risen can’t be conformed our Savior and Lord
The incapable death He destroyed with His might we are restored
Released us from the seal’s hold the resurrected Lord

ሂዱ ንገሩ አውሩ ለዓለም ተነሥቷል በዚህ የለም መድኃኔዓለም
ሞት የማይችለው የበረታ ኃያል ነው የማይረታ
ማኅተሙን የፈታ የትንሣኤው ጌታ

Hidu negeru awru le'alem tenes'twal bezih yelem medhanealem

entries.meaning: This mezmur proclaims the Resurrection of Jesus Christ and calls believers to spread the good news to the whole world. It celebrates Christ’s victory over death, the breaking of the seal of the tomb, and the restoration of humanity. The chant emphasizes salvation, the power of the Cross, and the joy of resurrection, encouraging bold testimony and praise. It reflects triumph, hope, and the eternal victory of Christ over sin and death.

entries.title: He is praised
entries.lyrics:
He is praised honored and glorified 
He shows me mercy when I abide   
I’ll praise Him give Him my offering
For my home is complete by my King 

Misgun new yetemesegene
Baryawun enen yikir yale
Yimesgen yimesgenilign
Gojoyen Amlak molalign

I know my Lord He gives for all generously
My void has been filled now receive my melody
For what I lacked you granted me its true
All will align for good by your virtue 

My sorrow He banished so that my joy perceives
The emptiness has passed you fulfilled all my needs
To give and take He has authority
To knock and ask is all He needs of me

I may be pushed and heaved but I won’t feel distress
You are my foundation my home is your fortress 
When you grant me tangibles to my eyes
Do not withhold from me your paradise

He gives from His vast wealth the sovereign gracious Lord
For every need we have His blessings have been poured
When we're deprived through Him we are restored
Praise to our God in our hearts He’s adored 

He is praised honored and glorified 
He shows me mercy when I abide   
I’ll praise Him give Him my offering
For my home is complete by my King 

Misgun new yetemesegene
Baryawun enen yikir yale
Yimesgen yimesgenilign
Gojoyen Amlak molalign

Baryawun enen yikir yale

entries.meaning: This mezmur is a song of gratitude and praise to God for His mercy, provision, and faithfulness. It expresses trust in God as the provider who fills every need and removes sorrow. The chant highlights a personal relationship with God, emphasizing reliance on Him as a foundation, gratitude for His blessings, and the joy that comes from His presence. It reflects themes of thanksgiving, faith, and divine provision.

entries.title: He who created you
entries.lyrics:
He who created you without blemish for the world’s salvation /2/
Blessed be God let His name praised 
Blessed be God let His let His name be praised
entries.meaning: This mezmur is a short praise focused on God’s perfect creation and His role in salvation. It glorifies God for creating without blemish and calls believers to continually bless and praise His holy name. It emphasizes divine perfection, salvation, and worship.

entries.title: He’s done wonders for me
entries.lyrics:
He’s done wonders for me the Lord God Almighty
Lifted me from the dust He never forgot me
ድንቅ አድርጎልኛል የሠራዊት ጌታ
አልረሳኝም አምላክ አነሳኝ ከትቢያ

He’s done wonders for me --- Without counting my sins
He’s done wonders for me --- He called me to His home
He’s done wonders for me --- While I had been worthless
He’s done wonders for me --- Told me my child come
He’s done wonders for me --- In His Second Coming
He’s done wonders for me --- To give me His kingdom

He’s done wonders for me the Lord God Almighty
Lifted me from the dust He never forgot me

He’s done wonders for me --- From the den of lions
He’s done wonders for me --- His shelter kept me safe
He’s done wonders for me --- In front of all my foes
He’s done wonders for me --- I’ve been given His grace
He’s done wonders for me --- How can I repay Him?
He’s done wonders for me --- His love can’t be replaced

He’s done wonders for me the Lord God Almighty
Lifted me from the dust He never forgot me

He’s done wonders for me --- All of my enemies
He’s done wonders for me --- Against me they had formed
He’s done wonders for me --- Who will come to rescue?
He’s done wonders for me --- Mockingly they had told
He’s done wonders for me --- Swiftly came to my aid
He’s done wonders for me --- From death I was restored

He’s done wonders for me the Lord God Almighty
Lifted me from the dust He never forgot me
ድንቅ አድርጎልኛል የሠራዊት ጌታ
አልረሳኝም አምላክ አነሳኝ ከትቢያ
entries.transliterationLyrics:
Dinq adrigoLignal yeserawit geta
Alresagnim Amlak anesagn ketbiya
entries.meaning: This mezmur is a testimony of God’s goodness and faithfulness. It expresses gratitude for the many wonders God has done, including forgiveness of sins, protection from danger, and restoration from suffering. The chant reflects a personal journey of being lifted from humility to grace, emphasizing God’s mercy, love, and saving power.

entries.title: Hosanna in Ariyam
entries.lyrics:
Holy Jerusalem you are at peace today 
He has entered your gates 
Your God leading the way 

Hosanna in Ariyam 
They sang unto their Lord
The children of Holy Jerusalem 

The city of David O Bethlehem rejoice 
The light of your people 
Has come hearing your voice 

With their praises they sang 
Hosanna to their King 
Within Jerusalem
Their hearts poured offerings 
 
Suffering of Jesus 
The pure of heart witnessed 
Their eyes welling with tears 
They all cried with sadness 

ሆሳዕና በአርያም / Hosanna be ariyam 
እያሉ ዘመሩ / Eyalu zemeru
ሕጻናት በኢየሩሣሌም / Hitsanat be Eyerusalem
entries.transliterationLyrics:
Hosanna be ariyam
Eyalu zemeru
Hitsanat be Eyerusalem
entries.meaning: This mezmur reflects the joyful and solemn entry of Jesus Christ into Jerusalem, where He was welcomed with praises of 'Hosanna' by the children and the people. It captures both the celebration of His kingship and the underlying sorrow, as His suffering was near. The chant highlights humility, praise, and the recognition of Christ as King and Savior.

entries.title: I call out your name
entries.lyrics:
With unshaken faith I call out your name
Pleading to Mary I won't fall in shame
I find my solace when I call to you
To you my reverence O Virgin is due

Simshin teriche meche afralew
Mariam biye meche ewedkalew
Yemitsnanabet simish newina
Dingil hoy lakrib lanchi misgana

In the land of foes swallowed by darkness
Living for ages trampled by meekness 
When the devil dragged me through misery 
The Savior of the world you brought for me

When I left the land of my father's place
Among the gentiles slavery I faced
You came to my aid bestowed your blessing
Mary with you I end my suffering

When my tent was filled with the wedding guests 
The ushers and hosts were in grave distress
When stricken without gifts to give in strife
Your swift aid steered me from the edge of life

My dreams like the clouds scattered in the breeze
Faded like a mist like feathers with ease
In my shortcomings I will still stand tall
In Theotokos hope answers the call

When my kin forsakes no one to confide
When this heartless world cast me out aside
With you fear subsides power dwells in me
Through the fiercest winds I will sail the sea

In the grasp of foes I found agony
Pain and suffering trials aplenty
I will never cease my praises to you
Dear Virgin Mary your marvels renew 

When tormentors loom shouting threats at me
Their words can't cease the love I hold for thee
In the scoffer's crowd your name I’ll decree
So my faith withstands shield me dear Mary

In the grasp of foes I found agony
Pain and suffering trials aplenty
I will never cease my praises to you
Dear Virgin Mary your marvels renew 

With unshaken faith I call out your name
Pleading to Mary I won't fall in shame
I find my solace when I call to you
To you my reverence O Virgin is due

Simshin teriche meche afralew
Mariam biye meche ewedkalew
Yemitsnanabet simish newina
Dingil hoy lakrib lanchi misgana
entries.transliterationLyrics:
Simshin teriche meche afralew
Mariam biye meche ewedkalew
Yemitsnanabet simish newina
Dingil hoy lakrib lanchi misgana
entries.meaning: This mezmur is a deep expression of faith and reliance on the intercession of the Holy Virgin Mary. It portrays a believer calling upon her in times of suffering, loneliness, and trials, finding comfort, strength, and hope. The chant highlights her role as Theotokos, the one who brings salvation through Christ, and emphasizes perseverance, faith, and unwavering devotion even in the face of hardship.

entries.title: I called unto my Dear Lord
entries.transliterationTitle: I Called Unto My Dear Lord
entries.lyrics:
I called unto my Dear Lord when my life was impaired 
I called unto my Father when I had none to spare
My anxiousness he calmed rescued me from despair 

When I appeal to Him He hears my desperate cry
His ears are attentive and open are His eyes
For He will rescue me from a life that is grim
I may be unworthy but still I will praise Him

Unable to walk on the path of righteousness 
The drowning waves of sin seemed to me tireless
When I appealed to Him with a regretful heart
He brought forth His mercy and pulled me from the dark

Just as you saved Noah rescue us from the storm
Shield us from the arrows coming in every form
My strength will be in you I won’t tremble in fear 
I will lean on the cross knowing my God is near 

With abundant mercy death was left in the past
He never left His child showing His love is vast
God received me with joy with His bright shining light
Who will stand against me with my Lord as my might
entries.meaning: This mezmur is a heartfelt prayer of calling upon God in times of distress and weakness. It expresses trust in God’s mercy, His readiness to hear prayers, and His power to rescue from sin, fear, and despair. The chant reflects repentance, dependence on God, and confidence in His protection, drawing parallels to biblical deliverance like Noah’s salvation. It emphasizes faith, repentance, and unwavering trust in God’s saving power.

entries.title: I found tranquility
entries.lyrics:
I found tranquility it is within Your home
Help me out of chaos I ask you Lord of hope

Thinking of all my sins my heart beats anxiously
Being far from you Lord my soul lives fearfully
Return me to Your grace I'll find my way back home
The one true holy peace I found in Your abode

For prayer liturgy I could not persevere
I stood before the crowd my praises not sincere
My endless temptations have dragged me far from You
Save me Emmanuel with faith that I once knew

Let my eyes pour their tears as they cry for my sins
May the rest of my days be spent in repentance
At the very hour the moment I'm redeemed
Thinking of Your love Lord I've fallen at Your feet

Ewnetegna selam yalew ke betih new
Erdagn alinawet eleminihalew
entries.transliterationLyrics:
Ewnetegna selam yalew ke betih new
Erdagn alinawet eleminihalew
entries.meaning: This mezmur is a deep expression of repentance and longing for peace found in God’s presence. It reflects the struggle with sin, spiritual distance, and the desire to return to a life of sincere worship. The chant emphasizes confession, humility, and the healing peace found in God’s house. It calls for restoration, tears of repentance, and renewed faith in Christ.

entries.title: I have no righteous deeds
entries.lyrics:
Enes bemigibare dekama hognalehu
Ezegnilegni Dingil Elemeneshalehu
Ezegnilegni Dingil Ematsenishalew

I have no righteous deeds, I have fallen to sin
Pray for me Saint Mary, see me with compassion
Plead for me Saint Mary, see me with compassion

So that I may escape, the eternal fire 
I call upon your name, mother of my Savior 

Medicine of Nahom, melody of David,
Manna of Elijah, come to us on a cloud 

You are my strength Mary, whenever I am weak 
Your intercession is, what my broken heart needs

My sins and my weakness, are causing me to stress
The mother of my Lord, pray for my forgiveness
entries.transliterationLyrics:
Enes bemigibare dekama hognalehu
Ezegnilegni Dingil elemeneshalehu
Ezegnilegni Dingil ematsenishalew
entries.meaning: This mezmur is a humble prayer of repentance and supplication to the Holy Virgin Mary. It expresses the believer’s recognition of sin and unworthiness, while seeking her intercession for mercy and forgiveness. The chant highlights Mary as a source of strength, comfort, and spiritual healing, emphasizing her role as the Mother of God who pleads on behalf of humanity. It reflects deep humility, repentance, and reliance on her compassion.

entries.title: I have seen all I need
entries.transliterationTitle: I Have Seen All I Need
entries.lyrics:
I have seen all I need (3) (Luke 2)
My Lord is here indeed

I was in disbelief of a virgin birth
But now I have seen this mystery here on earth
I waited for so long to see this prophecy
And now I have seen my Lord who came to rescue me

Without leaving His throne He came down from above
Although my sins were great He came to show His love
And now I can declare that I have been set free
My God is truly One and in One He is Three

My Lord Jesus Christ, my Savior and my King
I always worship Him it’s His name that I sing
The light of the world He’s all that I can see
The Word became flesh, and He came to save me

The Son becoming Man this holy mystery
No one can understand this sacred unity
Those who have eyes see! Those who have ears hear!
Christ has come to this world there’s nothing else to fear
entries.meaning: This mezmur proclaims the revelation of Christ through the Incarnation, reflecting the fulfillment of prophecy as described in Luke 2. It expresses the joy of witnessing the Savior, emphasizing the mystery of God becoming man, the Trinity, and the salvation brought through Jesus Christ. The chant highlights faith, divine love, and the assurance that Christ’s coming removes fear and brings eternal hope.

entries.title: I know the one who has saved me
entries.lyrics:
I know the one who has saved me
Jesus the Lord who died for me
Since I have no way to repay
I will give Him praise and glory

Banishing the darkness away
He is the one who shined His light
Because of Christ I am complete
I will follow Him day and night
I saw the brilliant light of God
Always ahead guiding me now 
His love brought me toward the church
I chose to worship Him and bow
I will give praise (3) For He loves me always

He gave me rest in His embrace
My corrupted life He maintains
Dying on the cross for my sins
I was healed while He was in pain
His tireless arms carry me
So that I may live in honor
For my salvation He came down
Humbly He laid in a manger 
I will give praise (3) For He loves me always

To glorify I will sing songs
On the cross Jesus shed His blood 
For the sake of His children’s lives
He gave His life for those He loved
This love brought Christ from the heavens
The humble God came for my gain
I became holy through the lamb
On Calvary the Lord was slain
I will give praise (3) For He loves me always

The King who wore a crown of thorns
Left His throne and glory for me
He guided me to my honor
With holy blood I was redeemed
Bowing His head He raised me up
His righteousness brightens my face
I will live the rest of my days
With Jesus Christ who gives me grace
I will give praise (3) For He loves me always

I know the one who has saved me
Jesus the Lord who died for me
Since I have no way to repay
I will give Him praise and glory

Yadanegnin awkewalew
Yemotelign Eyesus new
Silelelegn yemikeflew
Sitotaye misgana new
entries.transliterationLyrics:
Yadanegnin awkewalew
Yemotelign Eyesus new
Silelelegn yemikeflew
Sitotaye misgana new
entries.meaning: This mezmur is a personal declaration of salvation through Jesus Christ. It expresses gratitude for His sacrifice on the Cross, His love, and the transformation He brings into a believer’s life. The chant reflects themes of redemption, guidance, and devotion, emphasizing that Christ’s sacrifice cannot be repaid except through praise and faithful living. It highlights the journey from darkness to light and the commitment to follow Him always.

entries.title: I will forever sing for my Lady
entries.lyrics:
I will forever sing for my Lady 
For she has filled my home with homily
Because she’s visited me through this wretched life
I will praise the Virgin until the end of time 

From my sorrowful face she soothed away my tears 
I’m made to magnify her with songs and prayers 
With misery and pain I no longer suffer 
Into my home she came the mother of wonder

The disarray of life crumbling around me 
She cleared it all away restored my sanity 
She listens to my pleas my appeals and my cries
With her intercession my prayers will arise 

No longer do I see through a clouded vision 
My eyes have been healed by her hands of compassion 
When I appeal to her with sorrow and despair 
Immaculate is she, to the world I’ll declare 

Day and night I lament with a heavy burden 
From drowning she saved me so I won’t be saddened 
For she has pulled me free from the clench of my sins 
To her eternally I will raise up my hands 

I will forever sing for my Lady 
For she has filled my home with homily
Because she’s visited me through this wretched life
I will praise the Virgin until the end of time 

Yihew ezemiralew le Emebete
Ye fiqrua bereket gebto bete
Silegobegnechiw gosquala hiyweten
Ameseginalehu Dingil Emebeten
entries.transliterationLyrics:
Yihew ezemiralew le Emebete
Ye fiqrua bereket gebto bete
Silegobegnechiw gosquala hiyweten
Ameseginalehu Dingil Emebeten
entries.meaning: This mezmur is a heartfelt expression of love, gratitude, and devotion to the Holy Virgin Mary. It reflects the believer’s testimony of being comforted, healed, and restored through her intercession. The chant emphasizes her compassion, her role in lifting burdens and removing suffering, and the peace she brings into one’s life. It highlights continual praise, faith, and reliance on her as a source of spiritual strength.

entries.title: I will praise You
entries.lyrics:
I will praise You with Melodies God Almighty
Compositions will pour out of my heart for Thee
Singing to you completes me Lord it’s my treasure 
A gift given to glorify you my Father

From all that’s mine, I cannot find any worthy
Fill me oh Lord, all your gifts I’ll, return gladly 
You’re my wisdom, the foundation for all glory 
Bountiful are all the blessings, You’ve poured on me

My gracious Lord who tuned my heart to stand and praise
Receive my songs from the talents of which You gave 

Vast is the void I once called life, I’m left empty 
My hands deprived, offered none to The Almighty
Though I may live, a life tangled with transgression
The songs I sing, may You accept with compassion 

My gracious Lord who tuned my heart to stand and praise
Receive my songs from the talents of which You gave 

When you made me from the Earth's dust in your likeness
What work is there aside from praise it is priceless
Day and night I stand in the house of my father
To glorify tirelessly in Your honor

My gracious Lord who tuned my heart to stand and praise
Receive my songs from the talents of which You gave 

Ezra give me the instrument that’s Cherubic 
Grant me David your harp which is therapeutic 
Compositions and songs I learned from the clergy 
Touched by Your love I sing to You though unworthy

My gracious Lord who tuned my heart to stand and praise
Receive my songs from the talents of which You gave

Lamesginih yene Geta lamesginih
Liqegnilih yene Geta liqegnilih
Hiywete new zimare tirufate 
Yesetehegn endakebrih ante abate
entries.transliterationLyrics:
Lamesginih yene Geta lamesginih
Liqegnilih yene Geta liqegnilih
Hiywete new zimare tirufate 
Yesetehegn endakebrih ante abate
entries.meaning: This mezmur is a heartfelt offering of praise to God, recognizing that all gifts, talents, and abilities come from Him. It expresses humility, gratitude, and the desire to glorify God through song and worship. The chant reflects the believer’s acknowledgment of unworthiness while still offering praise with a sincere heart. It emphasizes worship as a purpose of life and highlights music as a divine gift used to honor God.

entries.title: I won't Forget
entries.lyrics:
I won’t forget what you did for me
Oh, Lord you went to hell for me
To save me from misery
Oh, Lord You went to hell for me
To defeat my adversary

I sold my soul to the serpent
I was stripped of being Your son
I was alone and deserted
Until my fate you reverted.
You saved the wretched one like me
Though I don’t deserve to be
You surrendered Yourself for me
With Your blood You set me free.

The weight of my sin was a burden
My condition seem to worsen
But then you came for the rescue
And paid my debt that I was due.
You saved the wretched one like me
Though I don’t deserve to be
You surrendered Yourself for me
With Your blood You set me free.

Never deserved, your divine gift
Up to the cross, you loved me first
And now I shout, from the rooftops
I’ll sing for You, I will not stop.
You saved the wretched one like me
Though I don’t deserve to be
You surrendered Yourself for me
With Your blood You set me free
entries.meaning: This mezmur is a powerful testimony of Christ’s sacrifice and redemption. It reflects deep gratitude for Jesus Christ, who conquered death and saved humanity from sin. The chant emphasizes the believer’s unworthiness contrasted with God’s unconditional love, highlighting the Cross, the shedding of His blood, and victory over the enemy. It calls for a life of continual praise in remembrance of His saving work.

entries.title: In That Very Hour
entries.lyrics:
In that very hour when Christ comes once again (2)
From the youth to the old (3) 
We will all face Him then

From the east to the west from the north to the south
When the winds are released (3) to rain down the chaos.

The heavens and the Earth when they start to align (2)                             
We will meet our maker (3) with nowhere left to hide

The Righteous to the right to the left the sinners (2)
With the sound of the horns (3) with the beat of the drums

The Earth disintegrates and the bones are gathered (2)  
It is overwhelming (3) when God’s judgment is served

The fear of the darkness the cries and the screaming (2)
For those who closed the door, to the light of the Lord, for those who closed the door, when Christ had been knocking

As they enter heaven the righteous by their works (2)
The sinners were sentenced (3) to the eternal curse

አቤት የዚያን ጊዜ ክርስቶስ ሲመጣ (2)
ትንሹም ትልቁም (3) መድረሻውን ሲያጣ
entries.transliterationLyrics:
Abet yezian gize Kristos simeta (2)
Tinishum tilikum (3) medreshawn siyata
entries.meaning: This mezmur reflects the Second Coming of Jesus Christ and the final judgment. It describes the gathering of all people, the separation of the righteous and the sinners, and the overwhelming reality of God’s judgment. The chant serves as a warning and a call to repentance, encouraging believers to remain faithful and prepared for the day when all will stand before Christ.

entries.title: In Your Temple O Lord
entries.lyrics:
In Your Temple O Lord You have raised me 
For my tongue you have taught me melody 
My God since birth it’s you I desire 
From pains of this world I have tired 

Be bete mekedese yasadekegn 
ye afayen tibebn yastemarkegn 
ye lijente amlak wedet sale 
kezi alem hasab kinde zale 

My home is a poorly made creation 
Quivering it is the foundation 
From the strength of my foes I am exhausted 
Alone I’m no longer supported

In Your Temple O Lord You have raised me 
For my tongue you have taught me melody 
My God since birth it’s you I desire 
From pains of this world I have tired 

Wasteful I have become rebellious 
My sins against you are numerous 
Clashing spiritually my path has blurred 
Who can reunite me with Your Word 

In Your Temple O Lord You have raised me 
For my tongue you have taught me melody 
My God since birth it’s you I desire 
From pains of this world I have tired 

You’re the peace that I long for all my years
I’ve written you in my heart with tears
The temptations of this world have chained me 
Restore me O Lord and hear my plea 

In Your Temple O Lord You have raised me 
For my tongue you have taught me melody 
My God since birth it’s you I desire 
From pains of this world I have tired 

Rebels enter your home though unworthy 
Touched by your love they receive mercy
From them your kind embrace did not depart 
Your warm compassion melted their heart

In Your Temple O Lord you have raised me 
For my tongue you have taught me melody 
My God since birth it’s You I desire 
From pains of this world I have tired 

Be bete mekedese yasadekegn 
ye afayen tibebn yastemarkegn 
ye lijente amlak wedet ale 
kezi alem hasab kinde zale
entries.transliterationLyrics:
Be bete mekedese yasadekegn
Ye afayen tibebn yastemarkegn
Ye lijente amlak wedet ale
Kezi alem hasab kinde zale
entries.meaning: This mezmur expresses a deep longing for God’s presence and the peace found within His holy temple. It reflects repentance, humility, and the desire to return to a life of sincere worship. The chant highlights God’s mercy in accepting the unworthy, restoring the broken, and giving purpose through praise. It emphasizes spiritual struggle, healing, and the transformation that comes from dwelling in God’s presence.

entries.title: Let us give praise
entries.lyrics:
Let us give praise (2) to the Almighty God
We praise Him for He is glorified (2)

We have crossed through the sea the waters have run dry
Upon our enemies triumphantly we rise
We were freed from their hold the chains of slavery
The bread that gives us life is truly heavenly

Trials that caused us fear abundant they all were
We have passed through them all with our Lord and Savior
Though we may lose our strength weakened by obstacles
Our salvation is God He makes all possible

We drank holy water that flowed from the boulder
We praise God Almighty who is full of wonder
Come all let us rejoice our lives have been redeemed
Our enemy Satan he has left in defeat
entries.meaning: This mezmur is a joyful proclamation of praise to God for His deliverance and salvation. It recalls biblical acts such as crossing the Red Sea and receiving life-giving provision, symbolizing God’s power to rescue His people. The chant emphasizes victory over enemies, overcoming trials through faith, and the ultimate defeat of Satan. It calls believers to rejoice and give thanks for God’s mighty works and redemption.

entries.title: Lord it is your grace
entries.lyrics:
Lord it is your grace that preserves me for all my days
Lord it is your grace that shepherds me for all my days 
Unto you I will chant these words to offer praise (2)

If I hide my talents and displease you   
If I don’t have my lamp nor wait for you
Don’t forget me my Lord won’t you forgive
With love save me from death so I can live
I’m never satisfied away from you 
I’m feeling so empty and hopeless too
I know you will offer me salvation 
I’ve come in repentance with devotion 

I have nothing else left you’re my last hope 
But you are kind to me that’s how I cope 
The weight on my shoulders is lighter now 
I received your mercy to you I bow
When I sing you this hymn in your temple 
When I tell others of your miracle   
Who am I to praise you it's not my place
Lord I marvel at your abundant grace 

I stopped putting my faith onto others 
Through mercy and kindness you're my helper 
With you sustaining me my life has healed
You are my medicine you are my shield 
I’m falling short of what you asked of me
The mystery on the Cross I failed to see 
My Lord disperse the fog that blocks my sight 
Then I’ll focus on you dear Jesus Christ 

Cherinetih new yaderesegn eske zare
Cherinetih new yetebeqegn eske zare
Lamesginih yene Geta be zimare (2)

ቸርነትህ ነው ያደረሰኝ እስከ ዛሬ

entries.meaning: This mezmur is a reflection on God’s grace and mercy as the sustaining force throughout a believer’s life. It expresses repentance, humility, and dependence on God as the only true source of hope, healing, and salvation. The chant highlights the struggle with sin, the desire to remain faithful, and the gratitude for God’s continuous guidance and protection. It emphasizes grace, repentance, and the transformative power of Christ.

entries.title: Melody of Yared
entries.lyrics:
Melody of Yared My Lady St Mary
a treasure from above
In which fitting words or in what language can I 
express to you my love

The heavens and the earth they speak of your marvels 
And all of creation testifies with novels 
A tale that is ceaseless, wondrous are your deeds
Oh My Virgin Mary to you none supersede  
Mother of all Saint Mary my strength and aid
You swiftly come when I call I’m not afraid 
Your miracles and wonders I have witnessed 
Zion Mary you have cured me of emptiness

The wisdom of our Lord unveiled by you Virgin 
Adam's children escaped the death that came from sin
It is you my mother the root of salvation 
Who became the ladder for all of creation
If the future's uncertain full of dismay
With you as my rod I cannot be led astray 
I’ll stand before you to give praise night and day
With humbleness I call you Mary I pray 

Generations as one they will stand to give praise
You’ve abolished the curse saved them from sorrow days
Adam and his children on Earth and in the skies 
He will sing songs to you may his prayers arise
The shadows of this dark world from me they part
Intercessions made by you have calmed my heart
When you’re with me Saint Mary I shall know peace
The clench from the pit of sorrow will be released

Melody of Yared My Lady St Mary
a treasure from above
In which fitting words or in what language can I 
express to you my love

entries.meaning: This mezmur is a poetic expression of devotion to the Holy Virgin Mary, inspired by the spiritual tradition of Saint Yared. It glorifies her as the Mother of God, the source of comfort, and the one through whom salvation was revealed. The chant emphasizes her intercession, her role in overcoming sin, and her place in all creation as a source of peace and hope. It reflects deep reverence, love, and admiration for her spiritual greatness.

entries.title: Mother of God
entries.lyrics:
Mother of God Virgin Mary
Day and night I will sing to thee
Your blessed son I'll glorify
Worship is due to The Most High

My heart it will arise tuned for praise I will sing
Through my humility I give my offering 
Zion you are near me Abraham's holy tent
Mother of shining light it’s you I lean against

O Green Meadow of Peace the one He had chosen
Ark of the covenant you're Isaiah's virgin
Queen of heaven and Earth standing on the Lord's right
Honor is due to her we will sing day and night

Dearest Virgin Mary the mother of us all
With your intercession save me so I don't fall
Help me cross through the flames keep me on the right path
Death and defeat will cease since I have you at last

Mother of God Virgin Mary
Day and night I will sing to thee
Your blessed son I'll glorify
Worship is due to The Most High

entries.meaning: This mezmur is a reverent hymn dedicated to the Holy Virgin Mary, the Mother of God. It glorifies her role as Theotokos, the chosen vessel through whom salvation came into the world. The chant highlights her intercession, her symbolic titles such as Ark of the Covenant and Queen of Heaven, and her closeness to God. It expresses devotion, praise, and reliance on her guidance and protection in the spiritual journey.

entries.title: My Benevolent Lord
entries.lyrics:
My Benevolent Lord
He Lifted me from dust when I was discarded
I praise Him safeguarded 

Closed off by Simon with a heavy discernment 
Though he looked down on me with self entitlement 
With a proclamation the Lord drew me near Him
I washed His feet with tears no longer feeling grim

Though my job was to tax collect and oversee
I was ordered by Christ to come down from the tree
His overwhelming grace I struggled to carry
For He has blessed my home Jesus Christ set me free

To be stoned by jurors when I was surrounded
They listed all my sins to see me convicted
Sin returned the verdict to each their own accord
Go home in peace He said my savior and my Lord

The God that I worship I know Him He is true
For all He's done for me with abundant virtue
Within my heart I bear His love and His statute 
I cannot leave His sight for He has made me new

Balewiletaye

Temesgen Getaye

entries.meaning: This mezmur is a testimony of transformation through the mercy and grace of Jesus Christ. It reflects on biblical moments such as the sinful woman who washed Christ’s feet and the calling of Zacchaeus, showing how Christ lifts the rejected and forgives sins. The chant emphasizes repentance, forgiveness, and the personal relationship with Christ, highlighting His compassion and power to renew a person’s life.

entries.title: My Lord's Appearance
entries.lyrics:
My Lord’s appearance disfigured and bruised
He bled out for me was whipped and abused
Took on my beatings and lashed in my place
His honor was stripped to clothe me in grace

ጌታዬ ሆነ ደም ግባት አልባ
ጀርባው በጅራፍ ስለእኔ ደማ
የእኔን መገረፍ እርሱ ተገርፎ
ክብር አለበሰኝ የእርሱ ተገፎ 

Trapped within hatred falsely convicted
The Lord gave me life while apprehended
The wicked see Him as an offender
But for me He is my liberator
You were chained to spare us
My Savior, Christ Jesus

My Lord’s appearance disfigured and bruised
He bled out for me was whipped and abused
Took on my beatings and lashed in my place
His honor was stripped to clothe me in grace

They had spat on Him mocked and insulted
Boldly ridiculed they were conceited
Encircled by them He refused to speak
To give me a voice stayed quiet and meek
You were slain to spare us
My Savior, Christ Jesus 

My Lord’s appearance disfigured and bruised
He bled out for me was whipped and abused
Took on my beatings and lashed in my place
His honor was stripped to clothe me in grace

In Him we found life poured from His punctures
Redeemed the watchers and the accusers
When His arms and legs were struck and were nailed
He had plucked my thorn from death we prevailed 
You were pierced to spare us
My Savior, Christ Jesus

My Lord’s appearance disfigured and bruised
He bled out for me was whipped and abused
Took on my beatings and lashed in my place
His honor was stripped to clothe me in grace

While bare and exposed He covered my shame
Out of His pure love vinegar He drank
With His stream of grace He had quenched my soul
He paid the great price and He made me whole
You were parched to spare us
My Savior, Christ Jesus 

My Lord’s appearance disfigured and bruised
He bled out for me was whipped and abused
Took on my beatings and lashed in my place
His honor was stripped to clothe me in grace

entries.meaning: This mezmur is a profound reflection on the suffering of Jesus Christ during His Passion. It vividly describes His humiliation, wounds, and sacrifice, emphasizing that He endured all suffering for the salvation of humanity. The chant highlights the exchange of grace, where Christ took on pain and shame to grant redemption and honor to believers. It calls for deep gratitude, reverence, and contemplation of the Cross and Christ’s love.

entries.title: My Strength and Power
entries.lyrics:
My strength and power my grace and honor(2)
The Trinity is forever and ever (2)

ኃይሌ ብርታቴ ክብሬ ሞገሴ /2/
የዘለዓለሙ የአብረሃሙ ሥላሴ /2/

My soul has been chosen to give praise and honor
To the Holy Spirit to the Son and Father 
Governing on the throne they're one as well as three 
Nothing's impossible to God the Almighty

My strength and power my grace and honor (2)
The Trinity is forever and ever (2)

Standing on the roadside helping all the needy
I will feed the hungry and care for the sickly
Make me like Abraham the saint I admire
Fill my home with blessings its what I desire

My strength and power my grace and honor (2)
The Trinity is forever and ever (2)

The Earth and the waters our God created
The works of our Lord by them I'm astounded
Come so you can witness the miracles on Earth
Rejoice for the barren Sarah has given birth

My strength and power my grace and honor (2)
The Trinity is forever and ever (2)

For He so loved the world He gave His only son
Tewahedo's mystery God and man became one

The Trinity is forever and ever (2)

entries.meaning: This mezmur glorifies the Holy Trinity, emphasizing God as the source of strength, honor, and grace. It proclaims the unity and mystery of the Father, Son, and Holy Spirit while also reflecting on God's creation, miracles, and love for humanity. The chant encourages a life of service, faith, and imitation of the righteous, highlighting the believer’s desire to live according to God’s will and give continual praise.

entries.title: My Home is Filled
entries.lyrics:
My home is filled with a great jubilee 
My tent is full with His praise and glory
For greater is the Lord that resides within me

Bedinkuane elilta mulu new

From the wrath of my enemy's anger
He shields me close my Lord and Protector
With compassion my Heavenly armor
Peace now resides in my home and shelter

My home is filled with a great jubilee 
My tent is full with His praise and glory
For greater is the Lord that resides within me

He leads the way for my path shining bright
In my fatigue for my knees he gives might
With victory He leads me from the front
His praise steers me for He’s never distant

My home is filled with a great jubilee 
My tent is full with His praise and glory
For greater is the Lord that resides within me

Though I have none He always longs for me
I have been filled no longer unworthy
Even in times full of uncertainty 
Joyous I am by the Lord Almighty

My home is filled with a great jubilee 
My tent is full with His praise and glory
For greater is the Lord that resides within me

I will not twine in lament no longer
I've filled my speech with wisdom and scripture
Though some days may remind me of my scar
I will pour songs from the praise reservoir

My home is filled with a great jubilee 
My tent is full with His praise and glory
For greater is the Lord that resides within me

entries.meaning: This mezmur expresses the joy and peace that come from God's presence within one's home and life. It emphasizes God's protection, guidance, and strength in times of struggle, reminding believers that true fulfillment comes from Him. The chant highlights victory over hardship, spiritual growth, and the transformation from sorrow to praise, encouraging constant trust in God's power and faithfulness.

entries.title: Nation of The Cross
entries.lyrics:
We are the nation of the cross
Our home is built in the heavens (Philippians 3:20)
We love Christ by choice not by force
Our message will be forgiveness 

As it is written for His sake we’re killed all day long (Romans 8:36)
And we desire to live with Him where we belong
If Christ is magnified in our death then let it be
For He's the one who died on the cross and set us free

We fear not those who kill the body but not the soul (Matthew 10:28)
But we fear God, He who can destroy both inside hell
When we live on this earth we know that we will suffer (John 16:33)
But Jesus overcame this world so we will cheer.

The gates of hell shall not prevail against our nation (Matthew 16:18)
It’s built upon the faith of rock that stays unshaken
We are not strange to witness the life of martyrdom
We know that God will honor their blood in His kingdom
entries.meaning: This mezmur proclaims the identity of believers as the people of the Cross, whose true home is in heaven. It emphasizes voluntary faith in Christ, a life rooted in forgiveness, and readiness to endure suffering for His sake. Drawing from scripture, it highlights courage in the face of persecution, trust in God's ultimate authority, and the victory of Christ over the world. The chant also honors martyrdom, affirming that those who sacrifice for their faith will be rewarded in God's eternal kingdom.

entries.title: O My Savior I Can Do All Through You
entries.lyrics:
O My Savior, I can do all through You
Emmanuel, I can do all through You
Only declare the word, and mountains will be moved
O My Savior, I can do all through You

Medhanialem yelem yemesanew
Ammanual yelem yemesanew
Ersu Kal Seenager Teraraw medanew
Medhanialem yelem yemesanew

I never thought I could pass this adversity
My bed was drenched with tears, as they rolled down my cheeks 
His strength is made perfect, in the face of weakness 
I passed through rough waters,  my burden became less

O My Savior, I can do all through You
Emmanuel, I can do all through You
Only declare the word, and mountains will be moved
O My Savior, I can do all through You

He’s the crown of my home, Advisor to my plans
The teacher of my soul, through the good and the bad
He didn’t forget me, in the day of my need 
He wiped my tears away, and filled my home with glee

O My Savior, I can do all through You
Emmanuel, I can do all through You
Only declare the word, and mountains will be moved
O My Savior, I can do all through You

Yesterday I was poor, I had nothing to claim 
I had not one title, and nothing to my name
But I’m made whole today, only by His mercy, 
Glory be to my God, He who never leaves me

O My Savior, I can do all through You
Emmanuel, I can do all through You
Only declare the word, and mountains will be moved
O My Savior, I can do all through You

He removes from my path, that which is not for me, 
And by His Will He gives, that which does profit me
All things were made by Him, and without Him was naught
Glory and Praise be to, our King Jesus Christ

O My Savior, I can do all through You
Emmanuel, I can do all through You
Only declare the word, and mountains will be moved
O My Savior, I can do all through You

Medhanialem yelem yemesanew
Ammanual yelem yemesanew
Ersu Kal Seenager Teraraw medanew
Medhanialem yelem yemesanew

entries.meaning: This mezmur expresses complete reliance on Christ as the source of strength, healing, and transformation. It highlights that through the Savior, all things are possible, even in times of weakness, sorrow, and hardship. The chant reflects a journey from suffering to joy, emphasizing God's mercy, guidance, and faithfulness. It encourages believers to trust in God's word, recognizing Him as the provider, protector, and sustainer of life, and to give glory to Jesus Christ in all circumstances.

entries.title: O the Archangel
entries.lyrics:
O the archangel (2) in Heaven Saint Michael
From the death and demise the wages of sin
Deliver us all with faith you strengthen

The tribe of Jacob ———— O Saint Michael
For the Israelites ———— O Saint Michael
Their guardian you are ———— O Saint Michael
With angelic might ———— O Saint Michael
The bearer of love and mercy a father to all hear our pleas

Your garment shimmers ———— O Saint Michael
Descend from above ———— O Saint Michael
Glistening with gold ———— O Saint Michael
With eyes of a dove ———— O Saint Michael
The bearer of love and mercy a father to all hear our pleas

Before your icon ———— O Saint Michael
I kneel for your grace ———— O Saint Michael
You give me comfort ———— O Saint Michael
In my darkest days ———— O Saint Michael
The bearer of love and mercy a father to all hear our pleas

Fetneh teradan atsnan bemnet

O Michael liqe melaikt
Behatiyat endanwedk endanimot

entries.meaning: This mezmur honors Saint Michael the Archangel as a powerful intercessor and protector. It reflects the belief in his role as a guardian of the faithful, especially in times of spiritual struggle and danger. The chant calls upon his strength to deliver believers from sin and harm, while also portraying him as compassionate and attentive to prayers. It expresses reverence, trust, and reliance on Saint Michael’s heavenly assistance and intercession before God.

entries.title: Oh All That You Have Done For Me
entries.lyrics:
Oh all that you have done for me with humility
I will give praise and worship you with jubilee
Emanuel for eternity I will submit to you my heart fully /x2/

The day that became night drowned by the darkness
Suffering through the marsh that took me under it
When I had thought I lost the ones supporting me
He truly stowed away my troubles endlessly

Our Lord and our strength our glory and power
Humiliated them with faith my wrongdoers
That you won’t forsake me finally I know truth
Above worldly calling I’ve committed to you

Few became abundant You fed the multitudes
To show my gratitude enough I can’t give you
Through the days and the nights, the one that shines the light
Glorious it all is your works and all Your might

Deserving you are for praise and admiration
For the humble of heart blessings, you have given
Impoverished I am not you are my fortune
My life is filled by you never again barren
entries.meaning: This mezmur is a song of gratitude and devotion to God for His mercy, provision, and faithfulness. It reflects on times of hardship, darkness, and loneliness, and how God delivered, protected, and restored the believer. The chant emphasizes humility, surrender, and lifelong commitment to Christ, recognizing Him as the true source of strength, abundance, and light. It encourages giving continual praise and acknowledging God’s blessings in both struggles and victories.

entries.title: On The Cross I Witnessed His Love
entries.lyrics:
On the Cross I witnessed His love /2/
Awakened I will sing for my Lord who’s above /2/

መስቀል ላይ ያየሁት ፍቅር /2/
ይቀሰቅሰኛል ለስሙ እንድዘምር /2/

He endured agony for they had struck His face
Even though He was pierced He gives unending grace
His love never wavered when they acted in wrath
Though He was chained and shoved He still protects their path

The saint now a sinner the truth became a lie 
When they boldly shouted saying that He should die
He was tested by man the Maker was demeaned
Christ was imprisoned and then Barabbas was freed

Although His back was whipped without any pity
He embraces them with forgiveness and mercy
In harshness they gave Him the bitter vinegar
And when they pierced His side out came blood and water

He wore a purple robe after they stripped His clothes
They placed it on His head the braided crown of thorns
O Hail King of the Jews they said mocking in strife
But the death of Jesus provided them with life

መስቀል ላይ ያየሁት ፍቅር /2/
ይቀሰቅሰኛል ለስሙ እንድዘምር /2/

entries.meaning: This mezmur reflects on the profound love of Christ revealed through His suffering and crucifixion on the Cross. It recounts the ظلم, mockery, and pain He endured, while emphasizing His unwavering mercy and forgiveness toward humanity. The chant highlights the sacrifice of Jesus as the source of life and salvation, inspiring believers to awaken spiritually and respond with praise and devotion. It calls the faithful to remember His love and remain steadfast in worship.

entries.title: Our Lord and King of Peace
entries.lyrics:
Yealemin bedel yesewin gif ayto

Tsidkin lemefetsem bedelin atfito
            Yeselamu meri yeselamu dagna
            Amlak tewelede tetemeke legna

Seeing the sins of the world oppression of mankind
He came down leaving 99 legions behind  
To fulfill righteousness and free us from our bind
            Our Lord and King of Peace and our judge full of grace
            Jesus Christ is truly born and baptized for our sake

The mighty King whom the highest heavens could not bear
For when He was born and baptized to make us pure
The mountains skipped like rams trembling out of fear 

            Our Lord and King of Peace and our judge full of grace
            Jesus Christ is truly born and baptized for our sake

The waters became troubled the Jordan turned to flee
Before the Lord’s face it could not stand to be seen
Just as David foretold within his prophecy

            Our Lord and King of Peace and our judge full of grace
            Jesus Christ is truly born and baptized for our sake

As he founded righteousness in the Jordan River 
Came from the clouds above the Heavenly Father 
Testifying to His Son’s true divine nature

            Our Lord and King of Peace and our judge full of grace
            Jesus Christ is truly born and baptized for our sake

Just as we find it written in the Holy Gospel 
The Holy Spirit came upon Him as a dove 
Pointing His wings to the Son and Father above

            Our Lord and King of Peace and our judge full of grace
            Jesus Christ is truly born and baptized for our sake

Witnessing the waters flee and the mountains leaping 
The heavens were opened and the clouds were speaking
On this day the world has seen this great mystery 

            Our Lord and King of Peace and our judge full of grace
            Jesus Christ is truly born and baptized for our sake
            Yeselamu meri yeselamu dagna
            Amlak tewelede tetemeke legna
entries.transliterationLyrics:
Yealemin bedel yesewin gif ayto

entries.meaning: This mezmur celebrates the mystery of Christ’s incarnation and baptism, emphasizing Him as the Lord and King of Peace. It reflects on His humility in coming down from heaven to save humanity and fulfill righteousness. The chant highlights the powerful events surrounding His baptism in the Jordan River, including the testimony of the Father and the descent of the Holy Spirit. It proclaims Christ’s divine nature, His role as judge full of grace, and the revelation of the Holy Trinity, calling believers to recognize and rejoice in this great mystery.

entries.title: Praise And Glory
entries.lyrics:
Praise and Glory is due unto Him
Hallelujah to our Lord and God /2/

He is Praiseworthy and worshipped by all with praiseful words

His great love and His vast mercy is broad and limitless
Our God He is trustworthy He will deliver us
The Provider for His creation Who is full of riches

We who are the Children of God let us all exalt Him
