entries.meaning: This mezmur is a powerful expression of praise and worship to God, acknowledging His greatness as Creator of heaven and earth. It highlights His mighty works, mercy, and faithfulness, emphasizing that all creation—both in heaven and on earth—glorifies Him. The chant calls believers to join the angels and the saints in exalting God's name, recognizing His saving power, compassion, and provision. It encourages a life centered on worship, gratitude, and reverence for God’s eternal glory.

entries.title: Saint Mary Has Ascended
entries.lyrics:
Saint Mary has ascended into paradise /2/
Angels surrounding her comfort her with delight 
She has risen from death, like her Son Jesus Christ 

Mariam aregalech wede Genet/2/
Yesemay Melaikt eyaregaguwat
Esuwam ende Lijwa tenestalech

To honor the prophecy, her father David told
To stand at the Lords right, wearing clothes of fine Gold 
Our mother ascended, with glory honor praise 
Through the seven heavens, where her Lord and Son dwells
Virgin Mary has ascended into paradise

Saint Mary has ascended into paradise /2/
Angels surrounding her comfort her with delight 
She has risen from death, like her Son Jesus Christ 

Let the heavens open, and let clouds bear witness
No one must be silent, when the Virgin ascends 
Let’s all venerate the mother of compassion 
Unites us with her son, through her intercession
Virgin Mary has ascended into paradise

Saint Mary has ascended into paradise /2/
Angels surrounding her comfort her with delight 
She has risen from death, like her Son Jesus Christ 

Your holy flesh that the, Lord  has taken from thee
Your holy and pure hands , carried divinity 
You are raised by your son, from the firm grasp of death
You are raised with honor, we have seen it through faith 
Virgin Mary has ascended into paradise

Saint Mary has ascended into paradise /2/
Angels surrounding her comfort her with delight 
She has risen from death, like her Son Jesus Christ 

Rejoice all apostles, our holy fathers
The Lord has ascended, St Mary your mother  
Take her linens with you, go tell to the nations
Speak how she has risen, with fervent devotion
Aregalech Mariam tenestalech

Mariam aregalech wede Genet/2/
Yesemay Melaikt eyaregaguwat
Esuwam ende Lijwa tenestalech
entries.transliterationLyrics:
Mariam aregalech wede Genet
Yesemay melaikt eyaregaguwat
Esuwam ende lijwa tenestalech
entries.meaning: This mezmur celebrates the ascension of the Virgin Mary into paradise, honoring her as the Mother of God who was raised in glory by her Son. It reflects the fulfillment of prophecy and highlights her exalted place at the right hand of the Lord. The chant emphasizes the joy of the angels, the witness of heaven, and the faith of believers in her resurrection and ascension. It also calls the faithful to venerate her and seek her intercession, recognizing her role in uniting humanity with Christ.

entries.title: She Is Born For Us All
entries.lyrics:
She is born for us all
The mother of God our Lord and Salvation

We give her our praise and our admiration our veneration
For she’s the hope of Adam and his children

ተወልዳለችና የጌታ እናት የኛ መድኃኒት

እናመስግናት እናወድሳት እንውደዳት
እንውደዳት የአዳምን ሕይወት
entries.transliterationLyrics:
Teweldalechna yegita enat yegna medhanit
Enamesgenat enawedsat enwededat
Enwededat yeAdamin hiwot
entries.meaning: This mezmur honors the birth of the Virgin Mary as the Mother of God and the source of salvation for humanity. It expresses deep reverence, praise, and love for her, recognizing her as the hope of Adam and all his descendants. The chant emphasizes her unique role in God’s plan of salvation and calls believers to venerate her with gratitude and devotion.

entries.title: The Almighty God is Gracious
entries.lyrics:
The Almighty God is gracious
He is my safe haven through times of hardships
እግዚአብሔር መልካም ነው
በመከራ ቀንም መሸሸጊያ ነው

When our father Adam was banished from Eden
Death had reigned over us making us feel beaten
As promised to save him so he would not falter
During his suffering He became his shelter

The Almighty God is gracious
He is my safe haven through times of hardships

Abraham left Harran his abode and homeland
With faith entered Canaan following God’s command
He put his hope in Him and he wasn’t deprived
Became vast like the sand his seed was multiplied

The Almighty God is gracious
He is my safe haven through times of hardships

In sorrow and trials times of my misery
From my anxious spirit He removes my worry
Evil serpent of old may rob me of my peace
But my God is gracious my pain came to a cease
Jesus Christ is gracious my pain came to a cease

The Almighty God is gracious
He is my safe haven through times of hardships
እግዚአብሔር መልካም ነው
በመከራ ቀንም መሸሸጊያ ነው
entries.transliterationLyrics:
Egziabher melkam new
Bemeqera qenim mesheshegiya new
entries.meaning: This mezmur praises God’s goodness and faithfulness as a refuge during times of hardship. It reflects on biblical examples such as Adam and Abraham to show how God remains a protector and provider throughout history. The chant emphasizes trust in God during suffering, highlighting His power to bring peace, remove anxiety, and fulfill His promises. It encourages believers to rely on His grace, recognizing Him as a constant shelter in both trials and blessings.

entries.title: The Angel
entries.lyrics:
The Angel of the Lord surrounds (Psalm 34:7)
Those who fear him and bounds (Psalm 34:7)
Them from any trap and from harm (Psalm 34:7)
Delivers them who rely on him, delivers them (Psalm 34:7)
 
He led the Israelites in the desert (Exodus 23:20)
As he comforted Daniel the prophet (Daniel 10:13-21)
Saint Michael is one of the Arch Angels
Who beseeches mercy for the faithful
 
He saved the three children in Babylon (Daniel 3:25)
Annunciated Saint Mary the Virgin (Luke 2)
In the presence of God he stands for all (Luke 1:19)
Saint Gabriel intercede so we don’t fall
 
The Angel of peace with a grace to heal
He gives health, the archangel Raphael
He gave Ezra the chalice of wisdom
Archangel Uriel guide us to freedom
  
The servants of the Lord are Archangels
They’re our shields in spiritual struggles
They are sent forth to minister for us (Hebrews 1:14)
For our salvation so we could be heirs
entries.meaning: This mezmur honors the holy archangels and their role in God’s divine plan as protectors, messengers, and servants of salvation. It draws from scripture to show how angels guard the faithful, deliver them from danger, and guide them spiritually. The chant highlights specific archangels—Michael, Gabriel, Raphael, and Uriel—emphasizing their intercession, healing, and guidance. It encourages believers to trust in God’s protection through His angels and to remain steadfast in faith during spiritual struggles.

entries.title: The God of Abraham
entries.lyrics:
The God of Abraham and Issac's true Savior
Paid our debt though His blood His love does not waver

With false testimonies on trial they accused Him
Sentencing Him to death our Lord who's without sin

Pilate tortured the Lord shackling Him in chains 
His love for the Romans made him side with their fame 

Holy Virgin Mary her tears she could not hold
As she watched on the Cross her son suffer unclothed

The feet that stood on a holy throne made of light
They were nailed for the sins He came to overwrite

The one that gave a crown of light to all the saints 
Wearing a crown of thorns on Calvary He stayed

The God of Abraham and Issac's true Savior
Paid our debt though His blood His love does not waver

የአብርሃም አምላክ የይስሐቅም ቤዛ
ወገኖቹን ሁሉ በራሱ ደም ገዛ
entries.transliterationLyrics:
YeAbraham amlak yeYishaq beza
Wegenochun hulu berasu dem geza
entries.meaning: This mezmur reflects on the sacrifice of Jesus Christ as the God of Abraham and Isaac, who redeemed humanity through His blood. It recounts His suffering, unjust trial, and crucifixion, emphasizing His sinless nature and unwavering love. The chant also highlights the sorrow of the Virgin Mary and the humility of Christ, who endured pain and humiliation to save mankind. It calls believers to remember His sacrifice and recognize the depth of His love and redemption.

entries.title: The Holy Apostle
entries.lyrics:
The Holy Apostle Saint Paul, the healer of the sick  
Your message is virtuous! 
You who received the crown,  
Beseech and pray for all of us,  
So God may save our souls 
Out of his mercy and his compassion, for the sake of his holy name! 
 
The Holy Apostle Saint Paul, the righteous,   
The healer of the sick, your message is virtuous! 
Beseech beseech and pray for all of us  
So God may save our souls /x2/
 
The persecutor who turned out to be God’s messenger
And a pharisee yet a teacher for non-believers 
We learned from you that God can change our past 
Through our life if we preach the crucified Christ  
 
You discount your status for the sake of those who are lost 
Enduring many trials, you preached Jesus at any cost  
For that you received a crown of righteousness
Became a role model for all the Christians   
 
When you’re sick God’s power became perfect in your weakness
His grace was sufficient that led you to strive for greatness  
Pray for us to be His chosen vessels
So we imitate you and all your lessons
entries.meaning: This mezmur honors Saint Paul the Apostle, highlighting his transformation from a persecutor of Christians to a powerful messenger of Christ. It praises his teachings, endurance through trials, and his role as a model of faith and perseverance. The chant emphasizes God's grace working through weakness and calls upon Saint Paul to intercede for believers, asking that they may follow his example in spreading the message of Christ and living a life of righteousness.

entries.title: The Lord Has Risen
entries.lyrics:
The Lord has risen let us all sing and shout 
Our God has risen spread the good news about 
With His might He destroyed the death and corruption 
We all have bore witness Jesus Christ has risen

The covenant He gave promised to save Adam 
From the verdict of death to pay off his ransom 
He came from the heavens crucified for our blame 
Erased death with His death witnesses we became

The Lord has risen let us all sing and shout 
Our God has risen spread the good news about 
With His might He destroyed the death and corruption 
We all have bore witness Jesus Christ has risen

Disciples of our Lord were truly in distress 
From the death of their God sorrow was in excess 
While they were gathering not knowing what to do 
He appeared to them and Jesus said peace to you

The Lord has risen let us all sing and shout 
Our God has risen spread the good news about 
With His might He destroyed the death and corruption 
We all have bore witness Jesus Christ has risen

Mary Magdalene was walking with misery 
Before the rise of dawn her God she went to see 
The one who was tortured called the King of the Jews 
She found Jesus risen and went to spread the news

Geta tenestual elil elil belu 
Amlak tenestual yemisirach belu 
Musina mekabir be haylu atfito 
Hulachin ayenew Kiristos tenesto
entries.transliterationLyrics:
Geta tenestual elil elil belu
Amlak tenestual yemisirach belu
Musina mekabir be haylu atfito
Hulachin ayenew Kiristos tenesto
entries.meaning: This mezmur proclaims the resurrection of Jesus Christ, celebrating His victory over death and corruption. It reflects on God’s promise to save humanity from death, fulfilled through Christ’s crucifixion and resurrection. The chant recounts the joy of the disciples and Mary Magdalene upon witnessing the risen Lord, emphasizing the spreading of the good news. It calls believers to rejoice, bear witness, and glorify Christ for His triumph and the salvation He has granted to all.

entries.title: The Old is Gone
entries.lyrics:
The old is gone the new has come (2nd Corinthians 5:17)
I have been made new in Christ
The old is gone the new has come
My King comes shining in the night
 
I left my father’s home (Luke 15:11)
I thought there’s something better
I was eating with swine
I lived like a beggar
Thinking of how evil that I have been
I went to my father without hesitation He forgave my sins
 
The sickness and the pain (Matthew 9:20)
All that I’ve endured
The blood from my body
Made me feel impure
I knew my savior would heal me I was sure
When I touched His garment twelve years of disease suddenly I was cured
 
Every day I watched (John 5:1-15)
Others being healed
Unable to move
Sadness I would feel
The Lord was my hope He came to rescue me
“Carry your bed” said the King, and I was set free
        
Dear Lord remember not
The sins I’ve committed
I’m done with my past
My heart I’ve submitted
My journey is not done so I go forward
I will not give up until I receive my heavenly reward.
entries.meaning: This mezmur focuses on transformation and renewal through Christ, emphasizing the message that the old life is gone and a new life has begun. Drawing from biblical stories such as the prodigal son, the woman with the issue of blood, and the healing at the pool, it illustrates God’s mercy, forgiveness, and healing power. The chant encourages repentance, perseverance, and faith, reminding believers to leave behind their past and continue forward in their spiritual journey toward salvation.

entries.title: The Purest of the Pure
entries.lyrics:
The garden enclosed my dove
There is no spot unto you or blemish
A fountain sealed from above
Intercede for us so we won't perish

The purest of the pure you are
The holiest of the holies by far
The shiniest of all the stars
The purest of the pure you are
The holiest of the holies by far
The shiniest of all the stars

The unborn bow down to you
The angels astonished by your meekness
And the elders praise you too
For you've begotten their Master Jesus

The purest of the pure you are
The holiest of the holies by far
The shiniest of all the stars
The purest of the pure you are
The holiest of the holies by far
The shiniest of all the stars

The adorned temple of God
The queen who will stand upon God's right side
When Eve sinned, we were exiled
But we're given a mother who's our pride

The purest of the pure you are
The holiest of the holies by far
The shiniest of all the stars
The purest of the pure you are
The holiest of the holies by far
The shiniest of all the stars

The unborn bow down to you
The angels astonished by your meekness
And the elders praise you too
For you've begotten their Master Jesus

The purest of the pure you are
The holiest of the holies by far
The shiniest of all the stars
The purest of the pure you are
The holiest of the holies by far
The shiniest of all the stars
entries.meaning: This mezmur praises the Virgin Mary as the purest and holiest among all creation. It highlights her role as the Mother of God, the sealed fountain, and the chosen vessel through whom Christ came into the world. The chant reflects on her humility, purity, and exalted position in heaven, honored by angels and saints alike. It also emphasizes her role as an intercessor for humanity, calling believers to seek her prayers and recognize her as the spiritual mother given to redeem what was lost through Eve.

entries.title: The Spirit
entries.lyrics:
The spirit within me that abides
You are my true teacher and my guide (John 14:26)
One with the Father and the Son
I worship You with devotion (2)

As Christ had promised You came (John 16:7)
Your temple is what my soul became
You kindled my heart like a flame (2)

Like the wind blows where it wishes (John 3:8)
With the abundance of Your riches
Come dwell in me Holy Spirit (2)

Holy Spirit descend from above (Matthew 3:16)
My paraclete immaculate dove
I am sealed with Your forgiving love (2) (Ephesians 4:30)

With you leading me to the truth (John 16:13)
I walk in Christ in the days of my youth
I will pour Your praise from my mouth (2)
entries.meaning: This mezmur is a prayer and praise to the Holy Spirit, acknowledging Him as the teacher, guide, and comforter of the believer. It reflects on the promise of Christ that the Spirit would come and dwell within His followers, transforming their hearts into His temple. The chant emphasizes the Holy Spirit’s role in leading believers into truth, granting wisdom, and sealing them with divine love. It encourages a life of devotion, surrender, and continual praise through the guidance of the Spirit.

entries.title: The Stars
entries.lyrics:
The Stars that shine in the darkness
Fountains of hope in the desert
Listen to the march of the martyrs
The heroes and fighters of the faith
Defenders of the Faith.

The first martyr Saint Stephen
While they were stoning you, you showed your mercy
For that You saw in the heavens the Holy Trinity
How you lived and died is Christ’s reflection
And your faith inspires our generation
 
Oh! Saint Stephen the first martyr defender of the faith.
The Stars…

The Armenian virgin Saint Arsema (Rasphime)
When the king lusted after your beauty.
He didn’t know you kept yourself in purity
You loved Christ till death with true affection
And your faith inspires our generation!

Oh! Saint Arsema the Armenian virgin defender of the faith
The Stars…

Saint George the Roman soldier
With your faith in Christ, you slayed the beast
In the heavens you sit, today with the high priest
You overcame trials and tribulations
And your faith inspires our generation!

O! Saint George the Roman soldier defender of the faith.
The Stars…

The thirty martyrs of Orthodox
Enemies of Christ they waged a war
You answered the call on the Libyan shore
You received your crown with persecution
And your faith inspires our generation!
O! Martyrs of the Libyan shore, defenders of the faith.
entries.meaning: This mezmur honors the saints and martyrs of the Orthodox faith, portraying them as shining stars who illuminate the darkness of the world. It recounts the lives and sacrifices of figures such as Saint Stephen, Saint Arsema, Saint George, and the modern martyrs, emphasizing their courage, purity, and unwavering faith in Christ. The chant inspires believers to follow their example, remain steadfast during trials, and uphold the faith with strength and devotion.

entries.title: The Virgin Gave Birth to Her Creator
entries.lyrics:
The Virgin gave birth to her creator
In swaddling clothes, laid in a manger
There was no room at all, a place to rest for guests 
St. Mary found a barn, to let her baby rest.

ድንግል ፈጣሪዋን ወለደችው 
በመጠቅለያም ጠቀቀለለችው
የለምና ስፍራ ለንግዶች ማረፊያ 
ግርግም አስተኛችው በከብቶች ማደርያ

She became overwhelmed, the city of David
The miracle of the birth of our great Lord
To announce the good news, the Angel descended.
It shined as a bright light, The glory of the child. 
This is a mystery, that cannot be expressed,
But in her heart Virgin Mary kept it guarded (2)

The wise men they arrived from the eastern nations 
They brought gold, incense, myrrh, to the child’s location 
They found the baby while carried by his mother
They proclaimed of this birth, to all of the others.
This is a mystery, that cannot be expressed,
But in her heart Virgin Mary kept it guarded (2)

The land of Judah was filled with glory and praise
A Galilean king has come from Nazareth
about this miracle, let Bethlehem proclaim
When she stopped the census, for heavenly worship 
This is a mystery, that cannot be expressed,
But in her heart Virgin Mary kept it guarded (2)

God became a human, he who could not be seen
As the second Adam, he came as a person
His wisdom mesmerized the young, and perfect bride 
The word had become flesh, it became unified
This is a mystery, that cannot be expressed, 
But in her heart Virgin Mary kept it guarded (2)

ድንግል ፈጣሪዋን ወለደችው 
በመጠቅለያም ጠቀቀለለችው
የለምና ስፍራ ለንግዶች ማረፊያ 
ግርግም አስተኛችው በከብቶች ማደርያ

In swaddling clothes, laid in a manger
There was no room at all, a place to rest for guests 
St. Mary found a barn, to let her baby rest.
entries.transliterationLyrics:
Dingil fetariwan weledachew
Bemeteqeleyaam teqeqelelechew
Yelemna sifra lengdoch marefiya
Girigm astengachew bekebtoch maderiya
entries.meaning: This mezmur celebrates the mystery of the Incarnation, where the Virgin Mary gave birth to her Creator, Jesus Christ. It reflects on the humility of His birth in a manger, the wonder of the angels, and the witness of the wise men. The chant emphasizes the divine mystery of God becoming human for the salvation of mankind, highlighting Mary’s role as the chosen vessel who kept these wonders in her heart. It calls believers to contemplate and glorify this profound mystery with reverence and faith.

entries.title: The Gracious and Adored Interceder
entries.lyrics:
The gracious and adored interceder
For our God you are truly His mother
You were chosen by the Trinity
So the human race takes after thee

Cheritu amalajitu hoy
Enatu neshina le Adonay
Be Silassie fit siltan yalesh
Ye bahiryachin memekya nesh

The one who was living in grief
Through your Son came peace and relief
When you gave birth to Jesus Christ
Adam gained his freedom and might

For those who cry out your name
And for those who will venerate
The Lord gave a covenant
To save us from the last judgement

The gracious and adored interceder
For our God you are truly His mother
You were chosen by the Trinity
So the human race takes after thee

He’s mindful our God from above
Calming His children with His love
For you stand on the Lord’s right side
For sinners plead and be their guide

For those who cry out your name
And for those who will venerate
The Lord gave a covenant
To save us from the last judgement

The gracious and adored interceder
For our God you are truly His mother
You were chosen by the Trinity
So the human race takes after thee

Descended from the highest realm
Adorned in the nature we claim 
Revealed in your arms our lady
Held divine for our query

For those who cry out your name
And for those who will venerate
The Lord gave a covenant
To save us from the last judgement

The gracious and adored interceder
For our God you are truly His mother
You were chosen by the Trinity
So the human race takes after thee

A treaty for restoration
O Zion for our salvation
Citizens of Heaven and Earth
All rejoice in the one you birthed 

For those who cry out your name
And for those who will venerate
The Lord gave a covenant
To save us from the last judgement

The gracious and adored interceder
For our God you are truly His mother
You were chosen by the Trinity
So the human race takes after thee

Cheritu amalajitu hoy
Enatu neshina le Adonay
Be Silassie fit siltan yalesh
Ye bahiryachin memekya nesh
entries.transliterationLyrics:
Cheritu amalajitu hoy
Enatu neshina le Adonay
Be Silassie fit siltan yalesh
Ye bahiryachin memekya nesh
entries.meaning: This mezmur honors the Virgin Mary as the gracious and beloved intercessor, chosen by the Holy Trinity to become the Mother of God. It emphasizes her role in the salvation of humanity through the birth of Jesus Christ, bringing freedom and restoration to mankind. The chant highlights her position at the right hand of God, continuously interceding for sinners and guiding believers. It calls the faithful to venerate her, seek her prayers, and rejoice in the divine mystery revealed through her, recognizing her as a bridge between heaven and earth.

entries.title: Theotokos
entries.lyrics:
Theotokos (4)
You’re truly full of wonder
You’re truly our mother
 
Isaiah saw this mystery (Isaiah 7:14)
About your virginity
When you read his prophecy
You showed your humility
                 You shall rejoice O God bearer! (Luke 1:28)
                 Since you gave us the redeemer
                 We lift our eyes to you and pray
                 For your compassion today

You are the true censer of gold (Exodus 30:1-9) (Leviticus 16:12)
Since you carried the burning coal
You are also like Aarons rod (Numbers 17:8)
Without seed you brought us God
                 O! Mary joy of angels
                 The Virgin foretold by the prophets
                 Rejoice for you have found favor (Luke 1:28)
                 God’s grace is yours forever
entries.meaning: This mezmur praises the Virgin Mary as Theotokos, the God-bearer, emphasizing the divine mystery of her role in the Incarnation. It draws from prophetic and biblical imagery, such as Isaiah’s prophecy, the golden censer, and Aaron’s rod, to show how she was chosen and prepared to carry Christ. The chant highlights her humility, purity, and favor before God, while calling believers to rejoice in her and seek her intercession with faith and devotion.

entries.title: There Is No One Like You
entries.lyrics:
There is no one like you Lord for me
Touching my heart your love set me free
Your laws have become my foundation
Everlasting you are salvation 

On my boat your teachings will be heard
My heart is a net to grasp your word 
When day turns to night your love will stand
The fish multiplied by your command

There is no one like you Lord for me
Touching my heart your love set me free
Your laws have become my foundation
Everlasting you are salvation 

You are dear to me God my treasure
My home and life are yours forever
You leveled the hills and now its clear
With you by my side there’s none to fear

There is no one like you Lord for me
Touching my heart your love set me free
Your laws have become my foundation
Everlasting you are salvation 

Today I regret those lonely days
Living in darkness against your ways
Now I know your love sweet as honey
What life is there without you for me

There is no one like you Lord for me
Touching my heart your love set me free
Your laws have become my foundation
Everlasting you are salvation 

You exiled them our enemies
We navigated adversities 
Tomorrow is a new day of praise
We’ll sing to our Lord and God always

Yalante lene man lihonegn
Geta hoy fikrih liben nekagn
HIgih nebere yafe mefcha
Yematiqeyer ante bicha
entries.transliterationLyrics:
Yalante lene man lihonegn
Geta hoy fikrih liben nekagn
Higih nebere yafe mefcha
Yematiqeyer ante bicha
entries.meaning: This mezmur expresses deep personal devotion and love for God, acknowledging that there is no one like Him. It reflects on transformation through His love, from a life of darkness to one of faith and purpose. The chant emphasizes God’s teachings as a foundation, His constant presence through all circumstances, and His power to overcome adversity. It encourages gratitude, repentance, and a lifelong commitment to praising and following God.

entries.title: There Is Power in Your Words
entries.lyrics:
There is power in Your words and victory in Your name
The mountains will tremble Emmanual I proclaim 
The seas will part again my Savior I proclaim

Dil ale besimih dil ale beqalih
Teraraw yinadal Ammanuel silih
Bahir yikefelal Medhanealem silih
Kadesh the great frightening desert

We have passed through without feeling thirst
You overthrew pharaoh to the sea
It is your name that has set us free 
It is you Lord who gave Liberty

The walls of Jericho fell with shouts
Enemies will fall without a doubt 
Marvelous how we took the city
It is by your will God almighty
It is by your will Lord almighty

Surpassing the trials and hardships  
The marah water changed from tasteless
If we receive manna from above
Its because you are the God of Love 

Today we will stand strong and alive
With your strength we continue to thrive 
From the fields we have gained royalty
Through your grace you changed our story

There is power in Your words and victory in Your name
The mountains will tremble Emmanual I proclaim 
The seas will part again my Savior I proclaim

entries.meaning: This mezmur proclaims the power of God’s word and the victory found in His name. It reflects on biblical events such as the parting of the Red Sea, the fall of Jericho, and God’s provision in the wilderness to demonstrate His might and faithfulness. The chant emphasizes that through God’s will, believers overcome trials, receive provision, and experience transformation. It encourages faith, strength, and trust in God’s power to guide and deliver His people.

entries.title: Through The Desert
entries.lyrics:
በብረሃ በሃሩሩ

Through the desert and the barren
Through the perils of the ocean
The archangel Saint Micheal is 
The one who saved Israel by his mighty wings

Saint Micheal is…… my support and my strength 
Saint Micheal is…… protection in the wild

entries.meaning: This mezmur honors Saint Michael the Archangel as a protector, guide, and intercessor for God's people. It recalls his role in delivering Israel through the wilderness and portrays him as a constant source of strength, support, and salvation in personal struggles. The chant emphasizes his powerful intercession, his care for believers throughout life, and his role in transforming lives through God’s grace. It encourages trust in Saint Michael’s protection and faith in God’s deliverance through him.

entries.title: To Him Who Sits on the Throne
entries.lyrics:
To Him who sits on the throne
The Lamb who is Slain to atone

Amen amen amen forever and ever
 
Let every creature in heaven and on earth
With thousands of angels and twenty-four elders
With the righteous and the four living beings
Praise the Conqueror and the King of kings/x2/

He became a man and, didn’t think it a loss

Let every knee bow, let every tongue praise

And no ears have heard, for that let us all sing/x2/
entries.meaning: This mezmur is a song of worship to Jesus Christ, the Lamb who was slain and now sits on the throne in glory. Drawing from the imagery of Revelation, it proclaims praise from all creation—angels, elders, and the righteous—honoring Him as King of kings. It reflects on His humility, sacrifice on the Cross, and ultimate victory, calling all to bow and glorify His name. The chant also looks forward to the promise of eternal life, renewal, and the heavenly kingdom prepared for believers.

entries.title: To Receive The Lord’s Love
entries.lyrics:
To receive the Lord’s love what have I done for Him
Although it’s not enough I’m offering this hymn
Your strength lifted me up aided my weakness then
You fixed this broken bridge so I can cross again

የወደደኝ ጌታ ምን አድርጌለት ነው

ሰባራውን ድልድይ ገጥመህ ያሳለፍከኝ

When I had nothing left begging in Jericho
Swallowed up by darkness I had nowhere to go 
The Lord of light had heard my cries and He drew near
With His hands He healed me my vision became clear

For many months and years I had been bedridden
No one came to my aid no support was given
My Lord and my Savior He came to me and said
Your sins are forgiven rise and carry your bed 

With twelve long years of pain blood flowing with no end
I was rejected with no one to call a friend
When Christ came near I touched the edge of His clothing
The power that came forth ended my suffering

I was a drunk and thief and an adulterer
I was the chief rebel and was a murderer
But I praise God because He changed my life around 
In His power and hope today I do abound 

የወደደኝ ጌታ ምን አድርጌለት ነው
ብዘምርለትም ያንስበታል ይኽው 
ከውድቀቴ አንስተህ ድካሜን ያገዝኸኝ
ሰባራውን ድልድይ ገጥመህ ያሳለፍከኝ

To receive the Lord’s love what have I done for Him
Although it’s not enough I’m offering this hymn
Your strength lifted me up aided my weakness then
You fixed this broken bridge so I can cross again
entries.transliterationLyrics:

entries.meaning: This mezmur reflects deep humility and gratitude for God’s unconditional love and mercy. It acknowledges that no human effort is enough to repay His grace, yet offers praise as a response. Through various biblical moments of healing and redemption, it shows how Christ restores the broken, forgives sins, and transforms lives. The chant emphasizes personal repentance, spiritual renewal, and the believer’s journey from darkness to light through the saving power of God.

entries.title: To Mother Mary
entries.lyrics:
To mother Mary/x2/
We will sing to her for eternity/x2/

The unblemished gate
Ezikiel’s written

Our foundation

Tender hearted dove
The bringer of peace
For my gloomy life
Shine your light on me
I will plead to your name
Virgin for my soul
Entrusted to you the one who consoles

It has no limits
Unspeakable truth
Through her I'm filled with joy
By Mary I'm proud
Calling to her name I will sing aloud

I ask her come to me
Through the night and day
She is by my side
Here with me to stay
Glorifying her

entries.transliterationLyrics:
Le Mariam
Enzemiralen lezelalem
entries.meaning: This mezmur expresses deep love, devotion, and reverence for the Virgin Mary as the Mother of God. It highlights her purity, her prophetic symbolism as the closed gate seen by Ezekiel, and her role as a source of peace and comfort. The chant emphasizes her intercession, portraying her as a constant presence who supports and consoles believers. It calls the faithful to honor her with continual praise and to entrust their lives to her care.

entries.title: Truly with Glory
entries.lyrics:
Truly with glory the Lord is risen

Through His death He subdued death and all corruption

The first fruit of the dead our Lord has risen

Truly with glory the Lord is risen

They could conceal the tomb and guard it with soldiers

entries.meaning: This mezmur proclaims the resurrection of Jesus Christ with joy and certainty, declaring His victory over death and corruption. It emphasizes that through His death, He destroyed death and became the first fruit of the resurrection. The chant highlights the futility of human attempts to contain Him, affirming His divine power and eternal nature. It calls believers to rejoice in His triumph, recognizing His sacrifice, boundless love, and the salvation granted through His resurrection.

Lezih yadereskegn ene manegn
entries.meaning: This mezmur expresses deep humility and gratitude, acknowledging that God’s love is undeserved yet freely given. It reflects on human weakness, sin, and failure, contrasted with God’s patience, mercy, and endless grace. The chant emphasizes the abundance of God’s blessings and the sacrifice He made to save humanity. It calls believers to recognize their dependence on Him, respond with gratitude, and remain in awe of His everlasting love.

entries.title: Until My Time Comes
entries.lyrics:

entries.meaning: This mezmur is a humble prayer of repentance and dependence on God. It expresses a deep desire for guidance, forgiveness, and protection throughout life until the believer returns to Him. The chant highlights God as the source of healing, peace, and righteousness, while acknowledging human sinfulness and the need for His mercy. It calls upon God to guard the soul, lead it on the right path, and ultimately grant salvation through His grace and love.

entries.title: Walk in the Light of God
entries.lyrics:

entries.meaning: This mezmur encourages believers to live in the light of God, embracing His love, peace, and guidance. It emphasizes trust in God during difficult times, reminding the faithful that fear, darkness, and sin are overcome through Christ. The chant highlights the saving power of Jesus through the Cross and calls for courage, prayer, and perseverance. It ultimately points to a life of hope, protection, and eternal joy for those who walk in God’s light.

entries.meaning: This mezmur is a simple proclamation of faith in the Holy Trinity—Father, Son, and Holy Spirit. It reflects the core belief of Christianity, affirming unity in the three persons of God. The chant emphasizes devotion, trust, and the foundational confession of faith shared by all believers.

entries.meaning: This mezmur is a liturgical song of praise offered to God, reflecting the unity of believers during worship. It highlights Christ’s sacrifice on the Cross, His resurrection, ascension, and promised second coming. The chant emphasizes standing together like angels in service to God, offering glory and praise with reverence. It calls the faithful to remember His redemptive work and remain steadfast in faith as they await His return.

entries.meaning: This mezmur is a joyful declaration of praise to the Holy Trinity—Father, Son, and Holy Spirit. It emphasizes that all creation, from angels to humanity, is called to worship and glorify God. The chant reflects on God's provision, protection, and guidance in daily life, encouraging continual praise both day and night. It calls believers to join in the heavenly worship, honoring God with gratitude and devotion.

entries.meaning: This mezmur reflects on the awe and fear of God’s power revealed during the final judgment. It describes the events of the end times, including the sounding of heavenly trumpets, the gathering of angels and saints, and the separation of the righteous from the wicked. The chant calls believers to humility, repentance, and readiness, recognizing that no one can stand before God without His mercy. It emphasizes the importance of living a righteous life and seeking God’s grace before that day arrives.

entries.meaning: This mezmur expresses deep devotion to the Virgin Mary as a compassionate refuge and intercessor. It portrays her as a loving mother who comforts the sorrowful, protects the weak, and brings hope to those in distress. The chant highlights her role in guiding believers through hardship and leading them to her Son, Jesus Christ. It emphasizes trust in her intercession and gratitude for her constant presence and care throughout life.

entries.title: With His Love The Lord Rescued Me

With His oil my wounds were lessened 
Abundant love has supported me 
He paid the price and He set me free

With His love the Lord rescued me
His graciousness my remedy
I will stand to glorify
Snatched from death I testify

ስለወደደኝ አዳነኝ
በቸርነቱ ፈወሰኝ
አቆመኝ ስለ ክብሩ
ከሞት ነጥቆኛል ፍቅሩ
entries.transliterationLyrics:
Sileweddedegn adanenegn
Bechernetu fewesegn
Aqomegn sile kibru
Kemot netqognal fikru
entries.meaning: This mezmur is a testimony of God’s saving love and mercy. It reflects on how the Lord rescues, heals, and restores the believer through His compassion and sacrifice. The chant emphasizes freedom from death and bondage, portraying Christ as the healer who brings peace in times of unrest. It calls believers to glorify God with gratitude, recognizing His love as the source of life, healing, and redemption.

entries.title: With His Mercifulness
entries.lyrics:
With His mercifulness with His loving kindness 
God will accept us He will forgive us (2) with His righteousness

Bekehalinetu beamlakinetu 
Yikir yilenal yitadegenal (2) becherinetu

How can we thank you Lord Glorious God Almighty 
You granted us this life to fulfill righteously 
It is always your will to save us with your grace 
You are love God indeed you cannot be replaced 
God will accept us He will forgive us (2) with His righteousness 

No one compares to Him in His love in His grace 
He is patient with us He endures for our sake 
He calls us to His way return to me He says 
Let us pray to Him now with humble repentance 
God will accept us He will forgive us (2) with His righteousness
entries.transliterationLyrics:
Bekehalinetu beamlakinetu
Yikir yilenal yitadegenal becherinetu
entries.meaning: This mezmur emphasizes God's mercy, loving-kindness, and willingness to forgive those who turn to Him. It reflects on His patience and enduring love, highlighting that salvation comes through His grace and righteousness. The chant calls believers to repentance, humility, and gratitude, encouraging them to return to God and trust in His compassion. It reminds the faithful that God’s mercy is greater than their sins and that He desires to save and restore them.

entries.title: With My Unfitting
entries.lyrics:
With my unfitting, undeserving soul
Let me sing for her, for the mother of lord
I have survived the earth, the dark and the
cold
Just thinking about her, her glory untold

The humbleness you showed is
immeasurable
You even cry for us, for the lowly people
You plea to save my soul, to relief my scares
My heart is drawn to you, Mary mother of
God

Because of this Mary, our affection is grand
We are begging you, to plead beseech for us
Your hands are stretched up to, towards our
God
To ask for forgiveness, you look up to the
lord

Seraphim Cherubim, angles blazing like fire
They couldn't touch our God, his grace and
power
Mary embraced the lord, you kissed and
nurtured him
Oh Virgin Mary, Your love is fair and rare
entries.meaning: This mezmur expresses deep humility and devotion toward the Virgin Mary, acknowledging the unworthiness of the believer while offering praise to her as the Mother of God. It highlights her compassion, intercession, and unique role in nurturing Christ, whom even angels could not approach. The chant emphasizes her love for humanity and calls upon her to plead for forgiveness and mercy, drawing believers closer to God through her care and intercession.

entries.title: Within the Sinner’s Tent
entries.lyrics:
Within the sinner’s tent the holy has appeared
Searching for your child my Lord you brought me near
Since you stand by my side my challenges have passed
The blessings you gave me are abundantly vast

ከኃጢያተኛው ድንኳን ቅዱሱ ቆመሀል
ልጄ የት ነው ብለህ እኔን ፈልገሀል
አንተ ስላለኸኝ ቀሏል መከራዬ
ለኔ ያደረከው ብዙ ነው ጌታዬ

Through your fulfilling grace you have completed me
This is what my heart longs to receive your mercy
My weakening has ceased the trials I have crossed
You found me the sinner like the coin that was lost

From the time you found me the fig as my cover
What I read from your face always I remember
Overcoming burdens you tell me I'll achieve
I can tell from your eyes you will not forget me

What do you see in me when knocking on my door
You are the Holy God while I am weak and poor
After this I won't soak my carpet full of tears
Enter into my home so your word I can hear

I know you are my Lord giving your love to all
Rescuing from lions deliver from the fall
I will sing to my God For my heart it has said
Jesus Christ our Savior you’re the heavenly bread

ከኃጢያተኛው ድንኳን ቅዱሱ ቆመሀል
ልጄ የት ነው ብለህ እኔን ፈልገሀል
አንተ ስላለኸኝ ቀሏል መከራዬ
ለኔ ያደረከው ብዙ ነው ጌታዬ
entries.transliterationLyrics:
Ke hatiategnaw dinkwan qidus qomehal
Lije yet new bileh enen felgehal
Ante selalekegn qelual mekeraye
Lene yaderekew bizu new getaye
entries.meaning: This mezmur expresses the profound mercy of Christ who seeks out and saves sinners. It reflects on God’s presence entering into the life of the unworthy, bringing comfort, healing, and transformation. The chant emphasizes personal redemption, comparing the believer to the lost coin found by the Lord. It highlights God’s compassion, His desire to dwell with His people, and the peace and strength He gives to overcome trials, calling believers to respond with gratitude and praise.

entries.title: Without You My Lord
entries.lyrics:
Without you my Lord I have no delight
Without you my God barren is my life
When I call Your name pleading for Your grace
The fires will calm from death I'll be saved

Yalante Geta desta mech ale
Yalante Amlake hiywet mech ale
Simihin terto yetematsene
Kesatim weta kemotim dane

As you held my hand and led me to You
I conquered the trials and misery too
I'll leave them behind the hardships will pass
My hunger and pain I'll forget at last

With Your blessings my chalice overflowed
I was unscathed by the rival's arrow
With thousands of words I will fill my mouth
Your marvelous deeds I will sing about

If my slayer walks through the night and day
While my savior God is with me to stay
Death lost its power I shall have no fear
I'll worship my King throughout all my years

The praise of angels became daily bread
Consumed by Your love my songs I'll extend
My despair is gone for hope is risen
The glory that's due to you I've given

Yalante Geta desta mech ale
Yalante Amlake hiywet mech ale
Simihin terto yetematsene
Kesatim weta kemotim dane
entries.transliterationLyrics:
Yalante Geta desta mech ale
Yalante Amlake hiywet mech ale
Simihin terto yetematsene
Kesatim weta kemotim dane
entries.meaning: This mezmur expresses complete dependence on God, declaring that life without Him is empty and without joy. It reflects on how God leads, protects, and delivers the believer from hardship, fear, and even death. The chant emphasizes gratitude for God’s blessings, strength in overcoming trials, and the peace that comes from His presence. It encourages continual praise, recognizing that true fulfillment and hope are found only in Him.

entries.title: You Are Without Fault
entries.lyrics:
You are without fault also you have no blemish upon you (x2)
Come forth from Lebanon O’ my sister and my bride
Come forth from Lebanon O’ my sister and bride (2)

Albeki newer we’eementeni bela’elaeki (x2) 
Tsaee imlebanos ehitiye merat (4) 

አልብኪ ነውር ወኢምንትኒ በላዕሌኪ(x2)
ፃኢ እምሊባኖስ እኅትየ መርዓት(x4)
entries.transliterationLyrics:
Albeki newer weementeni bela’elaeki
Tsaee imlebanos ehitiye merat
entries.meaning: This mezmur praises the purity and holiness of the Virgin Mary, describing her as without blemish and chosen by God. Drawing from the imagery of the Song of Songs, it portrays her as the bride called forth in glory. The chant emphasizes her spiritual perfection, her role in the divine plan, and her exalted place among all creation, inviting believers to honor her with reverence and devotion.

entries.title: You Who Saved From Fire
entries.lyrics:
You who saved from fire, Kirkos and his mother 
Save us as well /2/ Oh Archangel 
Save us as well /2/ Gabriel the archange
entries.meaning: This mezmur calls upon the Archangel Gabriel for intercession, recalling the deliverance of Saint Kirkos and his mother from fire. It expresses trust in the power of God working through His angels to save and protect the faithful. The chant is a brief yet powerful prayer asking for the same mercy, protection, and deliverance in times of danger and trial.

entries.title: Your Love Allured Me
entries.lyrics:
Your love allured me throughout my life and all my years (2)
God my salvation my medicine
Your love allured me throughout my life and all my years 

I have been purchased by Your precious blood
So I may serve You and leave this dark world 
Because of Your death I received my life
With Your suffering my burdens have died
For Your grace I’ll stand and sing songs for You 
Limited I am but still I’ll praise You 

The heavens and earth out loud they will shout
To honor and praise worship they will sprout
There is none like You and Your righteousness
I will sing to You my hearts offerings 
My fingers will play the strings of the harp 
With psalm day and night they will never stop

Scattered they all were when they saw Your grace 
For virtue they fell and they all obeyed 
Creator of the earth almighty You are
With love You conquer the hearts of us all 
Supported we are throughout our lives
By You our King with You by our side

ፍቅርህ ማረከኝ በሕይወቴ በዘመኔ (2)
እግዚአብሔር ለኔ መድኃኒቴ
ፍቅርህ ማረከኝ በሕይወቴ በዘመኔ

Fikrih marekegn behiywete bezemene (2)
Egziabher lene medhanite
Fikrih marekegn behiywete bezemene
entries.transliterationLyrics:
Fikrih marekegn behiywete bezemene
Egziabher lene medhanite
Fikrih marekegn behiywete bezemene
entries.meaning: This mezmur expresses the deep and transformative love of God that draws the believer throughout life. It reflects on redemption through Christ’s sacrifice, emphasizing that His blood has purchased and renewed the soul. The chant highlights continuous praise from both heaven and earth, recognizing God’s righteousness and unmatched glory. It encourages lifelong devotion, gratitude, and worship, acknowledging that God’s love sustains and guides believers in every moment.

entries.title: You’re the Soul for My Soul
entries.lyrics:
Lenefese nefeswa nesh, Lemengede fana
Endaltefa amnalew, Yanchi lij Negna

You’re the soul for my soul, and the Light of my path
I know I won’t perish i’m a child of Yours

Mothers of the righteous, in number they’re many
Before and after Christ, truly they are plenty
But Mary You are one, there is no one like You
You live on for ages, guiding us all to You

All those who call on you, all who honor your name
All who offer praises, your glory who proclaims
Truly he does profit, and truly does he gain
There is none who loses, there is none put to shame

Saint Gregory and the, righteous father St John
St Basil who is Great, and St. Athanasius 
Our teacher St. Ephrem and St George our father
All profited from you, calling you their Mother

Go and ask your Fathers, is what the Word proclaims
And when we did ask them, all of the righteous saints 
They did tell us of You, how you endlessly love
How you plead for mankind, saving us from above 

Gabriel from heaven, Elizabeth from Earth
Together they did preach, honor to Virgin birth 
Whoever pleads to You, calling upon Your name
He can only profit, no one is put to shame
entries.transliterationLyrics:
Lenefese nefeswa nesh, lemengede fana
Endaltefa amnalew, yanchi lij negna
entries.meaning: This mezmur is a deep expression of devotion to the Virgin Mary, recognizing her as a guiding light and spiritual mother. It emphasizes her unique role among all righteous women, highlighting that there is none like her. The chant reflects on the testimony of saints and prophets who honored her and benefited from her intercession. It encourages believers to call upon her with faith, affirming that those who seek her help will not be put to shame but will receive spiritual gain and guidance.
